messages.content:
You are ML Intern, an ML engineering assistant with 18 tools for training, fine-tuning, data processing, inference, and evaluation on the Hugging Face (HF) ecosystem. Your goal is to complete what the user requested with zero errors. You are fully autonomous — research, validate, implement, and deliver results without asking for unnecessary confirmation. # Your knowledge of HF libraries is outdated You do not know current APIs for TRL, Transformers, PEFT, Trackio, or other HF libraries. Your internal knowledge WILL produce wrong imports, wrong argument names, and wrong trainer configurations. Before writing any ML implementation code, start from the literature. The parallel research sub-agents can crawl papers, read their methodology sections, trace citation graphs, and extract the exact datasets and training recipes that produced published results. This is your primary advantage — use it. Your default workflow for any ML task: 1. Find the landmark paper(s) for the task or domain 2. Crawl their citation graphs to find recent downstream work 3. Read methodology sections (not abstracts) of the most promising papers — especially recent ones with strong results, lot of citations, and publications in high-impact conferences 4. Extract the recipe: what dataset, what training method, what hyperparameters produced those results 5. Validate and use those datasets for training ``` research({"task": "Literature crawl for [task]. Start from [paper/topic]. Crawl citation graph for recent downstream papers. Read their methodology sections (3, 4, 5) — extract the exact datasets, training methods, and hyperparameters that produced their best results. Attribute every finding to a specific result (e.g. 'Dataset X + method Y → 85.3% on benchmark Z'). Also find working code examples using current TRL/Transformers APIs.", "context": "User wants to [goal]. We need the best training recipe backed by published results."}) ``` The sub-agent knows how to use github_find_examples, github_read_file, explore_hf_docs, fetch_hf_docs, hf_inspect_dataset, and hf_papers (with citation_graph, read_paper, snippet_search, find_datasets). Be specific in your task description — name anchor papers or arxiv IDs when you have them. You can also call research tools directly (explore_hf_docs, github_read_file, etc.) for quick lookups. Skip research only for trivial non-code operations. # Mistakes you WILL make without research HALLUCINATED IMPORTS: You will import from modules that were renamed or removed. Example: old TRL trainer class names, deprecated Transformers APIs, wrong trackio config field names. Fix: read a current example script first. WRONG TRAINER ARGUMENTS: You will pass configuration arguments that don't exist in current trainer versions. Fix: fetch the actual trainer/config docs via explore_hf_docs + fetch_hf_docs. WRONG DATASET FORMAT: You will assume column names without checking. Training fails with KeyError. Fix: call hf_inspect_dataset or hub_repo_details and verify columns match the training method. DEFAULT TIMEOUT KILLS JOBS: You will leave timeout at the default 30m for training jobs. Training takes hours. The job gets killed and all progress is lost. Fix: set timeout based on model size (minimum 2h for any training). LOST MODELS: You will forget push_to_hub=True and hub_model_id in training config. Job storage is ephemeral — the filesystem is deleted when the job ends. Without push_to_hub, the trained model is permanently lost. BATCH FAILURES: You will submit all ablation/batch jobs at once without testing that one works first. All will fail for the same bug. Fix: submit ONE job first, verify it completes successfully, then submit the rest. SILENT DATASET SUBSTITUTION: When a requested dataset fails to load, you will silently switch to a different one without telling the user. Fix: if the requested dataset isn't available, tell the user and ask what to do. HARDCODED UNAVAILABLE PACKAGES: You will forget to install necessary packages like 'flash-attn' for flash_attention_2 or other packages that aren't automatically installed in the job environment. Fix: install necessary packages before running the job. SCOPE-CHANGING FIXES: Avoid at all costs! When you hit an error (especially OOM), you will try "creative" workarounds that change what the user asked for and/or change the training task itself — switching full SFT to LoRA on OOM, reducing max_length (silently truncates training data and changes what the model learns), disabling monitoring instead of fixing it. Do not do this. Fix errors with the minimal change that preserves the user's original request and are grounded in research and examples. If the original approach genuinely cannot work, explain why and ask the user for input before changing methods, sequence length, training approach or any other part of the task. # When writing ML code Required sequence before any training/fine-tuning/inference script: 1. Use `research` tool to find working examples, read docs, and get current API patterns 2. Validate dataset: hf_inspect_dataset or hub_repo_details to confirm column names and format 3. Validate model: hub_repo_details to confirm model exists, correct architecture/size/tokenizer Training logging: always set disable_tqdm=True, logging_strategy="steps", and logging_first_step=True in your TrainingArguments/SFTConfig so loss values are printed as plain text lines you can grep, not hidden inside tqdm progress bars. Dataset format requirements by training method: SFT: "messages", "text", or "prompt"/"completion" DPO: "prompt", "chosen", "rejected" GRPO: "prompt" # Trackio Trackio is natively integrated with Transformers Trainer and all TRL trainers — the built-in TrackioCallback handles init/log/finish. In TrainingArguments/SFTConfig/DPOConfig/GRPOConfig set: report_to="trackio" run_name="" # e.g. "sft_qwen3-4b_lr2e-5_bs128" project="" # keeps related runs grouped so you can compare them trackio_space_id="/mlintern-<8-char-id>" # creates a public dashboard Space `project` and `trackio_space_id` can also be set via TRACKIO_PROJECT / TRACKIO_SPACE_ID env vars. Alerts are how iterations decide what to change. Use trackio.alert(title, text, level) at every decision point in training. Levels: ERROR — stop and change approach (divergence, NaN, OOM) WARN — tweak hyperparameters (overfitting, early stopping, KL spike, reward collapse, slow convergence) INFO — milestones (training complete, target reached, checkpoint saved) Always include numeric values and an actionable suggestion in `text`, e.g. "loss=12.4 at step 200 — lr likely too high, try ×0.1". A future call must be able to parse it and act on it. To add alerts under Trainer/SFTTrainer/GRPOTrainer, pass a custom TrainerCallback via `callbacks=[...]` that calls trackio.alert() inside `on_log` (training metrics like loss, reward, kl) and `on_evaluate` (eval metrics — only available here, not in `on_log`). Keep each `if` simple: one metric, one threshold. Conditions stay easy to adjust between runs. Read alerts back between runs instead of parsing thousands of metric values. CLI — always use --json: trackio get alerts --project

--run --json trackio get alerts --project

--since --json # incremental polling trackio get run --project

--run --json trackio get metric --project

--run --metric --json trackio list runs --project

--json Python: api = trackio.Api(); api.alerts(

, run=, since=); api.runs(

) (each run has .name, .config, .alerts()). Drive the next config from prior alerts: diverged → lr × 0.1 overfitting → weight_decay × 10 or reduce capacity early stopping → lr × 0.5 or adjust schedule high accuracy → refine around current config Read prior config via api.runs(...).config and only mutate keys the alerts justify changing. # Data audit Before working with any dataset, audit it first. Do not assume you know what the data looks like — inspect it. Use hf_inspect_dataset to check: schema/columns, number of rows per split, value distributions for key columns, sample rows. Surface anything notable: class imbalance, missing values, unexpected formats, outliers, duplicate rows, etc. Looking at data is the best way to boost performance of any ML model plus it reduces the likelihood of failed jobs later. # When submitting a training job Before calling hf_jobs, output a pre-flight check: - Reference implementation: [which example you based this on] - Dataset format verified: [columns confirmed via hf_inspect_dataset/hub_repo_details] - push_to_hub=True and hub_model_id set - timeout: [value] (based on: [model size] on [hardware]) - Trackio monitoring included and deploying metrics to a public Space If you cannot fill in all items, stop and complete the missing steps first. For batch/ablation jobs: submit ONE job first. Check logs to confirm it starts training successfully. Only then submit the remaining jobs. Never submit all at once. Hardware sizing: 1-3B params: a10g-largex2 7-13B params: a100-large 30B+ params: l40sx4 or a100x4 70B+ params: a100x8 Note: a10g-small and a10g-large have the SAME 24GB GPU memory. The difference is CPU/RAM only. # Sandbox-first development For non-trivial scripts, develop and test in a sandbox before launching via hf_jobs: sandbox_create → install deps → write script → test with small run → fix errors → launch via hf_jobs at scale Use GPU sandbox (t4-small minimum) when testing code that uses CUDA, bf16, or model loading. CPU sandboxes cannot test GPU code paths. # When a task has 3+ steps Use plan_tool to track progress. One task in_progress at a time. Mark completed immediately after finishing. Update frequently to show the user what you're doing. # Error recovery When something fails: - Diagnose the actual error. Read the full error message and logs. - Do not retry the exact same thing. Identify what needs to change. - If an API/import error: check documentation for the correct API. - If an OOM error: (1) reduce per_device_train_batch_size and increase gradient_accumulation_steps proportionally to keep effective batch size identical, (2) enable gradient_checkpointing=True, (3) upgrade to larger GPU (a10gx4→a100→a100x4→a100x8). Do NOT switch training methods (e.g. SFT→LoRA) or reduce max_length — those change what the user gets. If OOM happens in sandbox, create a new sandbox with larger GPU hardware. - Never change the user's requested approach (training method, dataset, model, sequence length) without explicit approval. - If a tool call fails repeatedly for the same reason: stop and try a different approach. - Never silently substitute resources (datasets, models) — tell the user if something isn't available. # Task completion Before ending your turn, verify: - Did you actually DO what the user asked, not just explain what you would do? - If something failed: did you diagnose and fix it, or at minimum explain what went wrong and ask for user input? - For training jobs: did you include a working Trackio dashboard URL? Do not stop after describing what you plan to do. Continue calling tools until the task is verifiably done. Do not mark plan tasks as completed if they failed or are only partially done. # Autonomous / headless mode When running autonomously (no human in the loop), you MUST follow these rules: NEVER respond with only text. Every response MUST include at least one tool call. If you have nothing to do, check the plan, verify outputs or plan ahead. A text-only response ends the agent loop permanently — there is no human to re-prompt you. NEVER STOP WORKING. Do NOT decide you are "done" while time remains. The human is not watching — they expect you to use the ENTIRE time budget productively. Do NOT ask "should I continue?" or "is this a good stopping point?" — there is nobody to answer. You run until the timer expires or you are manually killed. Your workflow is a loop, not a checklist. Once you have a working result, KEEP ITERATING: LOOP UNTIL TIME RUNS OUT: 1. Research the approach (read docs, find examples, check current APIs) 2. Implement the solution (write code, set up training) 3. Train and evaluate 4. Save the model to the required output location / push it to Hugging Face Hub 5. Improve: tune hyperparameters, try different data, adjust the training recipe, try a different approach entirely 6. Go to step 1 HYPERPARAMETER TUNING: Do not tune hyperparameters by hand one-at-a-time. Write a script that launches a sweep over a grid of values (learning rate, epochs, batch size, etc.) and evaluates each run automatically. One well-designed sweep script beats ten manual experiments. If you run out of ideas: go back to the literature. Crawl citation graphs deeper — find papers you haven't read yet, read their methodology sections, extract new datasets or training tricks. Look for papers that cite your current approach and improved on it. Try combining recipes from different papers. Re-read the task prompt for angles you missed. Re-read the training logs for clues. There is always a paper you haven't read yet, and it probably has a better dataset. Check the remaining time periodically with the timer command specified in the task prompt. Budget your time: reserve at least 10 minutes at the end for final evaluation and model saving. The task is NOT done until: - The required output exists (e.g. final model, metrics reached, dataset updated etc) - You have evaluated the model and confirmed it works # Communication - Be concise and direct. No filler, no restating what the user said. - One-word answers when appropriate for simple questions. - Always include direct Hub URLs when referencing models, datasets, Spaces, or jobs. - For errors: state what went wrong, why, and what you're doing to fix it. - Do not over-explain or present elaborate option menus for simple tasks. When the user's intent is clear, act on it. Present options only when there's genuine ambiguity. - Use the `notify` tool only when the user explicitly asked for out-of-band notifications or when the task clearly requires reporting to a configured messaging destination. Do not use it for routine chat updates. # Tool usage - Execute multiple independent tool calls in parallel when possible. - HF_TOKEN is automatically available in job secrets — no need to include it extra. - For training monitoring: include Trackio in the script and provide the dashboard URL. - For private/gated datasets: HF_TOKEN is needed — it's auto-loaded into job secrets. # CLI / Local mode You are running as a local CLI tool on the user's machine. There is NO sandbox — bash, read, write, and edit operate directly on the local filesystem. Working directory: /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target Use absolute paths or paths relative to the working directory. Do NOT use /app/ paths — that is a sandbox convention that does not apply here. The sandbox_create tool is NOT available. Run code directly with bash. [Session context: Date=29-04-2026, Time=20:22:32.770, Timezone=CEST (UTC+02:00), User=morgan, Tools=18]

# ML Intern TandemFoilSet-Balanced Benchmark (FULL BENCHMARK RUN)

You are Hugging Face ML Intern running headlessly inside a Kubernetes Job on the pai2 cluster.

## Benchmark contract
- Target repository: https://github.com/morganmcg1/TandemFoilSet-Balanced.git
- Base ref: main
- Working branch: mlintern-pai2-24h-v2-r1. The startup script creates and checks out this branch before ML Intern starts. Work only from this branch. Ignore all other branches and pull requests in the target repo: do not inspect, check out, merge, cherry-pick, or use them for ideas, results, or history. Keep all commits and final pushes on this branch.
- ML Intern model: anthropic/claude-opus-4-7
- W&B project: wandb-applied-ai-team/senpai-v1-ml-intern
- Visible GPU budget for this launch: 8 GPU(s)
- Hard pod wall-clock budget: 24 hours
- The file `/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/deadline.txt` contains exact epoch deadlines.

This is a fresh independent replicate. Do not inspect, query, or reuse any previous ML Intern or Senpai W&B groups/runs, old replicate branches, harvested result files, or prior PRs as evidence for experiment choices or metrics. Use this branch's own work, the target repo benchmark docs, public reference material, and the runs you launch inside this pod.

## Target repo context
Before planning experiments or editing code, read the target repo's own benchmark docs: `program.md` and `data/SPLITS.md` if present. Treat those files as the source of truth for the CFD task, input/target shapes, split design, metrics, file boundaries, masking/padding rules, and any physics context. You may read `README.md` for setup/background, but do not mine historical leaderboard or prior-agent result sections for experiment ideas unless they are explicitly part of the benchmark contract. Also inspect `python train.py --help` before your first training run so you use the exact flags this repo exposes.

Do not try to follow Senpai's advisor/student PR workflow. You are one autonomous ML Intern launch on the branch above; use the repo docs to understand the benchmark, then choose your own experiment strategy.

## Compute policy
Training compute must stay inside this local pai2 pod. Do not launch Hugging Face Jobs, Sandboxes, Spaces, or any other remote compute for training or evaluation. Hugging Face Hub session upload/logging is fine as long as training remains local.

You may decide how to use the visible GPUs: one experiment at a time, multiple parallel one-GPU jobs, or a mixed strategy. If you run jobs in parallel, explicitly pin each subprocess with `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES` so two training jobs do not accidentally use the same GPU.

## Training budget
There is no Senpai per-experiment timeout for this comparison. The environment sets `SENPAI_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=720` only to prevent the TandemFoil training script from using the previous 30-minute cap. The hard budget is the Kubernetes 24-hour launch kill switch. A 30-minute per-experiment runtime was used elsewhere as an initial baseline; you may follow it, go shorter, or go longer if your strategy benefits.

When you run the target training script, treat this as the default full command shape unless you deliberately choose otherwise:

```bash
python train.py --epochs 999 --agent ml-intern-r1 --wandb_group mlintern-pai2-24h-v2-r1 --wandb_name "mlintern-pai2-24h-v2-r1/<short-description>"
```

Use `--epochs 999` as the no-epoch-limit default. If you pick a different epoch count, document why.

## Objective and reporting
Optimize TandemFoilSet-Balanced under the target repo's own rules. Prioritize `val_avg/mae_surf_p` while preserving paper-facing `test_avg/mae_surf_p` reporting when final candidates are evaluated.

Before finishing, commit and push to `mlintern-pai2-24h-v2-r1`:
- The code/config changes you want credited to this replicate.
- `research/MLINTERN_SUMMARY.md` with your strategy, commands, W&B run/group names, best validation metric, test metric if available, GPU usage strategy, and next recommendation.
- `research/MLINTERN_RESULTS.jsonl` with one JSON object per meaningful run when possible.

Do not delete ML Intern's local `session_logs/` directory or temporary command-output logs. The pod entrypoint will harvest those conversation/tool-call artifacts into `research/` before shutdown.

1	<!--
     2	SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2026 CoreWeave, Inc.
     3	SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
     4	SPDX-PackageName: senpai
     5	-->
     6	
     7	# TandemFoilSet
     8	
     9	Research target for CFD surrogate modelling on the [TandemFoilSet](https://openreview.net/forum?id=4Z0P4Nbosn) dataset. Given tandem-airfoil geometry and flow conditions, predict the full velocity `(Ux, Uy)` and pressure `p` field at every mesh node.
    10	
    11	The baseline is a [Transolver](https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.02366) with physics-aware attention over irregular meshes. Beat it.
    12	
    13	## Codebase
    14	
    15	- `train.py` — trainer. Transolver model, training loop, validation, end-of-run test evaluation, W&B model-artifact upload. **Primary editable entrypoint.**
    16	- `data/loader.py` — `SplitDataset`, `TestDataset`, `load_data`, `load_test_data`, `pad_collate`. **Read-only during normal experiment PRs.**
    17	- `data/scoring.py` — MAE accumulation shared by val and test. **Read-only.**
    18	- `data/prepare_splits.py` — materializes per-sample `.pt` files on the PVC from `data/split_manifest.json`. Organizer script, run once. **Read-only.**
    19	- `data/generate_manifest.py`, `data/split_manifest.json`, `data/SPLITS.md` — split design and manifest. **Read-only.**
    20	- `instructions/prompt-advisor.md`, `instructions/prompt-student.md` — senpai role prompts.
    21	- `pyproject.toml` — runtime deps. Add any new package you need in the same PR that uses it.
    22	
    23	## Data
    24	
    25	Pre-processed samples live on the PVC at `/mnt/new-pvc/datasets/tandemfoil/splits_v2/`:
    26	
    27	```
    28	splits_v2/
    29	├── train/000000.pt ...           Each: {x: [N,24], y: [N,3], is_surface: [N]}
    30	├── val_single_in_dist/...        Random holdout from single-foil (sanity check)
    31	├── val_geom_camber_rc/...        Unseen front foil camber M=6-8 (raceCar)
    32	├── val_geom_camber_cruise/...    Unseen front foil camber M=2-4 (cruise)
    33	├── val_re_rand/...               Stratified Re holdout across all tandem domains
    34	├── test_*/...                    4 test splits (x + is_surface; y hidden)
    35	├── .test_*_gt/                   Hidden {y, is_surface} joined in by `load_test_data`
    36	├── stats.json                    Normalization stats (x_mean, x_std, y_mean, y_std)
    37	└── meta.json                     Split counts, domain groups
    38	```
    39	
    40	### Input features (x, 24 dimensions)
    41	
    42	| Dims | Feature |
    43	|------|---------|
    44	| 0-1   | Node position (x, z) |
    45	| 2-3   | Signed arc-length (`saf`) |
    46	| 4-11  | Distance-based shape descriptor (`dsdf`) |
    47	| 12    | Is surface node (0/1) |
    48	| 13    | `log(Re)` |
    49	| 14    | AoA foil 1 (radians) |
    50	| 15-17 | NACA foil 1 (camber, position, thickness) |
    51	| 18    | AoA foil 2 (radians, 0 for single-foil) |
    52	| 19-21 | NACA foil 2 (0,0,0 for single-foil) |
    53	| 22    | Gap between foils (0 for single-foil) |
    54	| 23    | Stagger between foils (0 for single-foil) |
    55	
    56	### Targets (y, 3 dimensions)
    57	
    58	| Channel | Description |
    59	|---------|-------------|
    60	| 0 | `Ux` — velocity x-component |
    61	| 1 | `Uy` — velocity z-component |
    62	| 2 | `p`  — kinematic pressure (p/ρ, m²/s²) |
    63	
    64	### Loading data
    65	
    66	```python
    67	from data import load_data, load_test_data
    68	
    69	train_ds, val_splits, stats, sample_weights = load_data()
    70	# train_ds[i]                              → (x, y, is_surface)
    71	# val_splits["val_single_in_dist"][i]      → (x, y, is_surface)
    72	# stats                                    = {x_mean, x_std, y_mean, y_std}
    73	# sample_weights                           → for balanced domain sampling
    74	
    75	test_splits = load_test_data()
    76	# test_splits["test_single_in_dist"][i]    → (x, y, is_surface)
    77	# y is joined in from the hidden .test_*_gt/ dirs so the end-of-run test
    78	# evaluation can compute MAE in the trainer without a separate submission step.
    79	```
    80	
    81	### Batching and padding
    82	
    83	Samples have **variable mesh sizes** (74K to 242K nodes). The dataloader pads each batch to the largest sample using `pad_collate`, which returns:
    84	
    85	```python
    86	x, y, is_surface, mask = batch
    87	# x:          [B, N_max, 24]  — padded with zeros
    88	# y:          [B, N_max, 3]   — padded with zeros
    89	# is_surface: [B, N_max]      — False for padding
    90	# mask:       [B, N_max]      — True for real nodes, False for padding
    91	```
    92	
    93	**The `mask` tensor is critical.** Model output includes predictions for padding positions — these must be excluded from loss and metrics. The baseline `train.py` and `data/scoring.py` handle this correctly. If you write a custom loss or pooling, always use `mask` to ignore padding.
    94	
    95	### Dataset domains
    96	
    97	The training data spans three physical domains with different mesh sizes and flow regimes (counts are exact from `meta.json`):
    98	
    99	| Domain | Train samples | Mesh nodes (mean) | Description |
   100	|--------|---------------|-------------------|-------------|
   101	| RaceCar single | 599 | ~85K  | Single inverted airfoil with ground effect, Re 100K–5M, AoA -10° to 0° |
   102	| RaceCar tandem | 457 | ~127K | Dual inverted foils (raceCar Parts 1+3), Re 1M–5M, AoA -10° to 0° |
   103	| Cruise         | 443 | ~210K | Tandem freestream foils (cruise Parts 1+3), Re 110K–5M, AoA -5° to +6° |
   104	
   105	The three domains are **equally weighted** in training via a balanced sampler (`sample_weights` from `load_data`) — otherwise raceCar single would dominate.
   106	
   107	### Physics context
   108	
   109	Each sample is a 2D CFD simulation over an overset mesh with up to 3 zones:
   110	
   111	```
   112	┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   113	│  Zone 0 — coarse background (full domain)       │
   114	│                                                 │
   115	│       ┌──────────────┐   ┌──────────────┐       │
   116	│       │  Zone 1      │   │  Zone 2      │       │
   117	│       │  (dense,     │   │  (dense,     │       │
   118	│       │   foil 1)    │   │   foil 2)    │       │
   119	│       └──────────────┘   └──────────────┘       │
   120	│                                                 │
   121	└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   122	```
   123	
   124	The raw dataset contains per-node boundary IDs (foil surfaces vs. interior / inlet / outlet / walls); in these preprocessed samples those are collapsed to a single boolean `is_surface` (True on either foil surface, False everywhere else). The model does not see a foil 1 vs. foil 2 distinction — use dims 18–23 of `x` (foil 2 AoA, NACA, gap, stagger) to tell tandem from single.
   125	
   126	**Value ranges.** Target magnitudes vary dramatically across domains (summary from the single-file val holdouts):
   127	
   128	| Source split | Re range | y range (min, max) | Avg per-sample y std | Max per-sample y std |
   129	|--------------|----------|--------------------|----------------------|----------------------|
   130	| `val_single_in_dist` (raceCar single) | 104K–5M | (-29,136, +2,692) | 458 | 2,077 |
   131	| `val_geom_camber_rc` (raceCar tandem P2, M=6-8) | 1.0M–5M | (-10,312, +2,228) | 377 | 1,237 |
   132	| `val_geom_camber_cruise` (cruise tandem P2, M=2-4) | 122K–5M | (-7,648, +2,648) | 164 | 506 |
   133	
   134	Within every split, high-Re samples drive the extremes — per-sample y std varies by an order of magnitude even inside one domain. The model must handle both low-Re (small values) and high-Re (extreme values) regimes.
   135	
   136	### Parameter space
   137	
   138	- **Reynolds number**: ~100K to ~5M across the whole corpus. Training sees the full range; there is no intentional OOD Re slice.
   139	- **NACA profiles**: 4-digit codes encoding camber (M), position (P), thickness (T). Features 15–17 are normalized to [0, 1]. Single-foil (file 0) sweeps M=2–9 plus a small "specials" cohort that encodes non-NACA foils as `(0, 0, 0)`. Tandem Parts partition the front-foil camber with no overlap:
   140	  - raceCar tandem: P1 M=2–5, P2 M=6–8 (**held out**), P3 M=9 + 5 non-NACA specials
   141	  - cruise tandem:  P1 M=0–2, P2 M=2–4 (**held out**), P3 M=4–6
   142	- **AoA**: raceCar -10° to 0° (inverted, negative loading); cruise -5° to +6°. Per-foil for tandem.
   143	- **Gap / stagger**: tandem geometry parameters — gap ~[-0.8, 1.6], stagger ~[0.0, 2.0]. Both are 0 for single-foil samples.
   144	
   145	### Splits
   146	
   147	Four validation tracks test different generalization axes, each paired with a held-out test split on the same axis:
   148	
   149	| Track | What it tests |
   150	|-------|----------------|
   151	| `val_single_in_dist` / `test_single_in_dist` | Sanity: random holdout from single-foil |
   152	| `val_geom_camber_rc` / `test_geom_camber_rc` | Unseen front foil camber M=6-8 — geometry interpolation (raceCar) |
   153	| `val_geom_camber_cruise` / `test_geom_camber_cruise` | Unseen front foil camber M=2-4 — geometry interpolation (cruise) |
   154	| `val_re_rand` / `test_re_rand` | Stratified Re holdout across all tandem domains — cross-regime generalization |
   155	
   156	Val is 4 × 100 samples; test is 4 × 200 samples. See `data/SPLITS.md` for the full split design rationale, per-file counts, and the reasoning behind each holdout.
   157	
   158	## Model contract
   159	
   160	The baseline `train.py` plugs a Transolver into this interface:
   161	
   162	- **Input**: `{"x": tensor [B, N, 24]}` — batch of **normalized** node features.
   163	- **Output**: `{"preds": tensor [B, N, 3]}` — predicted `[Ux, Uy, p]` in **normalized** space (the `(y - y_mean) / y_std` space).
   164	
   165	Normalization is applied outside the model:
   166	
   167	```python
   168	# Normalize inputs
   169	x_norm = (x - stats["x_mean"]) / stats["x_std"]
   170	# Normalize targets for loss computation
   171	y_norm = (y - stats["y_mean"]) / stats["y_std"]
   172	# Model predicts in normalized space
   173	pred = model({"x": x_norm})["preds"]  # [B, N, 3]
   174	# Denormalize for MAE computation
   175	pred_phys = pred * stats["y_std"] + stats["y_mean"]
   176	```
   177	
   178	Keep this contract intact — `data/scoring.py` assumes predictions are given in normalized space and denormalizes with `y_std * pred + y_mean` before MAE is taken.
   179	
   180	## Metrics
   181	
   182	All metrics in `data/scoring.py` are computed in the original (denormalized) target space, in float64, with per-sample skipping for non-finite ground truth. The same helpers are used for validation during training and for the end-of-run test evaluation so the numbers are apples-to-apples.
   183	
   184	**Primary ranking metric.** Equal-weight mean surface-pressure MAE across the four splits:
   185	
   186	- val: `val_avg/mae_surf_p` — averaged across the four validation splits
   187	- test: `test_avg/mae_surf_p` — averaged across the four test splits
   188	
   189	Aggregation is global over all valid surface nodes in the split:
   190	
   191	```
   192	mae_surf_p(S) = Σ_{valid surface nodes in S} |p_pred - p_true| / n_surf_nodes
   193	```
   194	
   195	**Per-split diagnostics** (logged every epoch for each val split, and once at the end for each test split):
   196	
   197	- `{split}/mae_surf_{Ux, Uy, p}` — surface MAE per channel, physical units
   198	- `{split}/mae_vol_{Ux, Uy, p}` — volume MAE per channel, physical units
   199	- `{split}/loss`, `{split}/vol_loss`, `{split}/surf_loss` — normalized-space losses used for training
   200	
   201	**Checkpoint selection.** Best checkpoint = lowest `val_avg/mae_surf_p`. That checkpoint is the one evaluated on the test splits at the end of training, and the one saved as a W&B model artifact (`model-<wandb_name-or-agent>-<run_id>` with aliases `best` and `epoch-N`).
   202	
   203	Lower is better. For paper-facing numbers, the decision-driving quantity is `test_avg/mae_surf_p`. Surface pressure accuracy matters most.
   204	
   205	## Constraints
   206	
   207	- **VRAM**: GPUs have 96 GB. Don't OOM — meshes can reach 242K nodes.
   208	- **Timeout**: each training run is capped by `SENPAI_TIMEOUT_MINUTES` (wall clock) and `--epochs`. Do not override these.
   209	- **Simplicity**: all else being equal, simpler is better. A small improvement that adds ugly complexity is not worth it.
   210	- **Data loaders are read-only.** Don't change the interface in `data/`. If you need a different sampler or feature transform, do it in `train.py`.
   211	- **No new packages** outside of `pyproject.toml`. If you need one, add it in the same PR that uses it.
   212	
   213	## Roles
   214	
   215	Research is coordinated through GitHub PRs with an advisor/student model. GitHub Issues are used for communication with the human researcher team. See `instructions/prompt-advisor.md` and `instructions/prompt-student.md`.

1	# TandemFoilSet-Balanced Split Design 
     2	
     3	## Totals
     4	
     5	| | Samples | % |
     6	|---|---|---|
     7	| Train | 1499 | 55.5% |
     8	| Val (4 x 100) | 400 | 14.8% |
     9	| Test (4 x 200) | 800 | 29.6% |
    10	| **Total** | **2699** | **100%** |
    11	
    12	## Split Allocation
    13	
    14	The goal behind the split is to be able to say:
    15	- The model is good/bad at generalizing to unseen geometries (train on low + high camber, val/test on moderate)
    16	- The same model works across different Reynolds numbers for both race car and cruise (train on low, med, high Re, val/test on low, moderate, high) - random split across all Re numbers
    17	- General single-foil random split as a sanity check
    18	
    19	
    20	| File | Train | Val | Test | Holdout Reason |
    21	|------|-------|-----|------|----------------|
    22	| 0 (single) | 599 (67%) | 100 (11%) | 200 (22%) | Random holdout for in-dist sanity check |
    23	| 1 (rc P1) | ~225 (75%) | ~25 (8%) | ~50 (17%) | Partial: stratified Re holdout for val_re_rand |
    24	| 2 (rc P2) | 0 (0%) | 100 (33%) | 200 (67%) | **Full holdout**: unseen front foil camber M=6-8 |
    25	| 3 (rc P3) | ~225 (75%) | ~25 (8%) | ~50 (17%) | Partial: stratified Re holdout for val_re_rand |
    26	| 4 (cruise P1) | ~225 (75%) | ~25 (8%) | ~50 (17%) | Partial: stratified Re holdout for val_re_rand |
    27	| 5 (cruise P2) | 0 (0%) | 100 (33%) | 200 (67%) | **Full holdout**: unseen front foil camber M=2-4 |
    28	| 6 (cruise P3) | ~225 (75%) | ~25 (8%) | ~50 (17%) | Partial: stratified Re holdout for val_re_rand |
    29	
    30	
    31	## Val/Test Splits
    32	
    33	All splits are **balanced**: 100 val + 200 test each. Scored as equal-weight average Surface MAE.
    34	
    35	| Split | Source | Selection | What It Tests |
    36	|-------|--------|-----------|---------------|
    37	| val_single_in_dist | File 0 (random) | Random holdout | Sanity check: can the model reproduce single-foil flow it has seen? |
    38	| val_geom_camber_rc | File 2 (full holdout) | File-level | Camber interpolation: train sees M=2-5 and M=9, must predict M=6-8 |
    39	| val_geom_camber_cruise | File 5 (full holdout) | File-level | Camber interpolation: train sees M=0-2 and M=4-6, must predict M=2-4 |
    40	| val_re_rand | Files 1+3+4+6 (stratified) | Every 4th sample sorted by Re | Cross-regime generalization: random sample across full Re range from all tandem training domains |
    41	
    42	## Design Rationale
    43	
    44	**Why full file holdouts for geometry?**
    45	Files 2 and 5 contain front foil shapes with NACA camber values (M=6-8, M=2-4) that are completely absent from training. This is the cleanest possible separation — no leakage, no soft boundaries.
    46	
    47	**Why stratified Re sampling instead of threshold cuts?**
    48	We want to show a single model works across all Reynolds numbers. A stratified random holdout across the full Re range tests whether the model learns a smooth Re-dependent representation.
    49	
    50	**Why no AoA split?**
    51	Simplicity. AoA extremes (deep stall, near-zero loading) are interesting but add complexity without a clean physical boundary. AoA is implicitly tested within each split since AoA is uniformly distributed across the holdout samples.
    52	
    53	## Dataset: File-by-File
    54	
    55	| File | Name | Domain | n | Avg Nodes | Front Foil NACA M | Rear Foil | Re | AoA | Ground |
    56	|------|------|--------|---|-----------|-------------------|-----------|-----|-----|--------|
    57	| 0 | raceCar single | Land, inverted | 899 | 85K | M=2-9 + 5 specials | None | 100K-5M | [-10, 0] | Yes |
    58	| 1 | raceCar tandem P1 | Land, inverted | 300 | 124K | M=2-5 | 30 shared | 1M-5M | [-10, 0] | Yes |
    59	| 2 | raceCar tandem P2 | Land, inverted | 300 | 129K | **M=6-8** | 30 shared | 1M-5M | [-10, 0] | Yes |
    60	| 3 | raceCar tandem P3 | Land, inverted | 300 | 130K | M=9 + CH10,E423,FX74,LA5055,S1210 | 30 shared | 1M-5M | [-10, 0] | Yes |
    61	| 4 | cruise tandem P1 | Aerial, freestream | 300 | 210K | M=0-2 | 30 shared | 110K-5M | [-5, +6] | No |
    62	| 5 | cruise tandem P2 | Aerial, freestream | 300 | 209K | **M=2-4** | 30 shared | 110K-5M | [-5, +6] | No |
    63	| 6 | cruise tandem P3 | Aerial, freestream | 300 | 211K | M=4-6 | 30 shared | 110K-5M | [-5, +6] | No |
    64	
    65	**Key structural facts:**
    66	- Within each domain (raceCar/cruise), all tandem Part files share the **same 30 rear foils**. Only the front foil varies between Parts.
    67	- Front foil NACA camber (M) is partitioned across Parts with no overlap.
    68	- raceCar uses inverted foils with ground effect (slip wall BC). Cruise has freestream on all boundaries.
    69	- File 3 contains 150 NACA 9xxx + 150 non-NACA specials. Non-NACA foils return (0,0,0) from parse_naca().
    70

1	"""Train a Transolver surrogate on TandemFoilSet.
     2	
     3	Four validation tracks with one pinned test track each:
     4	  val_single_in_dist      / test_single_in_dist      — in-distribution sanity
     5	  val_geom_camber_rc      / test_geom_camber_rc      — unseen front foil (raceCar)
     6	  val_geom_camber_cruise  / test_geom_camber_cruise  — unseen front foil (cruise)
     7	  val_re_rand             / test_re_rand             — stratified Re holdout
     8	
     9	Primary ranking metric is ``avg/mae_surf_p`` — the equal-weight mean surface
    10	pressure MAE across the four splits, computed in the original (denormalized)
    11	target space. Train/val/test MAE all flow through ``data.scoring`` so the
    12	numbers are produced identically.
    13	
    14	Usage:
    15	  python train.py [--debug] [--epochs 50] [--agent <name>] [--wandb_name <name>]
    16	"""
    17	
    18	from __future__ import annotations
    19	
    20	import os
    21	import subprocess
    22	import time
    23	from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass
    24	from pathlib import Path
    25	
    26	import simple_parsing as sp
    27	import torch
    28	import torch.nn as nn
    29	import torch.nn.functional as F
    30	import wandb
    31	import yaml
    32	from einops import rearrange
    33	from timm.layers import trunc_normal_
    34	from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, WeightedRandomSampler
    35	from tqdm import tqdm
    36	
    37	from data import (
    38	    TEST_SPLIT_NAMES,
    39	    VAL_SPLIT_NAMES,
    40	    X_DIM,
    41	    accumulate_batch,
    42	    aggregate_splits,
    43	    finalize_split,
    44	    load_data,
    45	    load_test_data,
    46	    pad_collate,
    47	)
    48	
    49	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    50	# Transolver model
    51	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    52	
    53	ACTIVATION = {
    54	    "gelu": nn.GELU,
    55	    "tanh": nn.Tanh,
    56	    "sigmoid": nn.Sigmoid,
    57	    "relu": nn.ReLU,
    58	    "leaky_relu": nn.LeakyReLU(0.1),
    59	    "softplus": nn.Softplus,
    60	    "ELU": nn.ELU,
    61	    "silu": nn.SiLU,
    62	}
    63	
    64	
    65	class MLP(nn.Module):
    66	    def __init__(self, n_input, n_hidden, n_output, n_layers=1, act="gelu", res=True):
    67	        super().__init__()
    68	        act_fn = ACTIVATION[act]
    69	        self.n_layers = n_layers
    70	        self.res = res
    71	        self.linear_pre = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(n_input, n_hidden), act_fn())
    72	        self.linear_post = nn.Linear(n_hidden, n_output)
    73	        self.linears = nn.ModuleList(
    74	            [nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(n_hidden, n_hidden), act_fn()) for _ in range(n_layers)]
    75	        )
    76	
    77	    def forward(self, x):
    78	        x = self.linear_pre(x)
    79	        for i in range(self.n_layers):
    80	            x = self.linears[i](x) + x if self.res else self.linears[i](x)
    81	        return self.linear_post(x)
    82	
    83	
    84	class PhysicsAttention(nn.Module):
    85	    """Physics-aware attention for irregular meshes."""
    86	
    87	    def __init__(self, dim, heads=8, dim_head=64, dropout=0.0, slice_num=64):
    88	        super().__init__()
    89	        inner_dim = dim_head * heads
    90	        self.dim_head = dim_head
    91	        self.heads = heads
    92	        self.softmax = nn.Softmax(dim=-1)
    93	        self.dropout = nn.Dropout(dropout)
    94	        self.temperature = nn.Parameter(torch.ones([1, heads, 1, 1]) * 0.5)
    95	
    96	        self.in_project_x = nn.Linear(dim, inner_dim)
    97	        self.in_project_fx = nn.Linear(dim, inner_dim)
    98	        self.in_project_slice = nn.Linear(dim_head, slice_num)
    99	        torch.nn.init.orthogonal_(self.in_project_slice.weight)
   100	        self.to_q = nn.Linear(dim_head, dim_head, bias=False)
   101	        self.to_k = nn.Linear(dim_head, dim_head, bias=False)
   102	        self.to_v = nn.Linear(dim_head, dim_head, bias=False)
   103	        self.to_out = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(inner_dim, dim), nn.Dropout(dropout))
   104	
   105	    def forward(self, x):
   106	        B, N, _ = x.shape
   107	
   108	        fx_mid = (
   109	            self.in_project_fx(x)
   110	            .reshape(B, N, self.heads, self.dim_head)
   111	            .permute(0, 2, 1, 3)
   112	            .contiguous()
   113	        )
   114	        x_mid = (
   115	            self.in_project_x(x)
   116	            .reshape(B, N, self.heads, self.dim_head)
   117	            .permute(0, 2, 1, 3)
   118	            .contiguous()
   119	        )
   120	        slice_weights = self.softmax(self.in_project_slice(x_mid) / self.temperature)
   121	        slice_norm = slice_weights.sum(2)
   122	        slice_token = torch.einsum("bhnc,bhng->bhgc", fx_mid, slice_weights)
   123	        slice_token = slice_token / ((slice_norm + 1e-5)[:, :, :, None].repeat(1, 1, 1, self.dim_head))
   124	
   125	        q = self.to_q(slice_token)
   126	        k = self.to_k(slice_token)
   127	        v = self.to_v(slice_token)
   128	        out_slice = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(
   129	            q, k, v,
   130	            dropout_p=self.dropout.p if self.training else 0.0,
   131	            is_causal=False,
   132	        )
   133	
   134	        out_x = torch.einsum("bhgc,bhng->bhnc", out_slice, slice_weights)
   135	        out_x = rearrange(out_x, "b h n d -> b n (h d)")
   136	        return self.to_out(out_x)
   137	
   138	
   139	class TransolverBlock(nn.Module):
   140	    def __init__(self, num_heads, hidden_dim, dropout, act="gelu",
   141	                 mlp_ratio=4, last_layer=False, out_dim=1, slice_num=32):
   142	        super().__init__()
   143	        self.last_layer = last_layer
   144	        self.ln_1 = nn.LayerNorm(hidden_dim)
   145	        self.attn = PhysicsAttention(
   146	            hidden_dim, heads=num_heads, dim_head=hidden_dim // num_heads,
   147	            dropout=dropout, slice_num=slice_num,
   148	        )
   149	        self.ln_2 = nn.LayerNorm(hidden_dim)
   150	        self.mlp = MLP(hidden_dim, hidden_dim * mlp_ratio, hidden_dim,
   151	                       n_layers=0, res=False, act=act)
   152	        if self.last_layer:
   153	            self.ln_3 = nn.LayerNorm(hidden_dim)
   154	            self.mlp2 = nn.Sequential(
   155	                nn.Linear(hidden_dim, hidden_dim), nn.GELU(),
   156	                nn.Linear(hidden_dim, out_dim),
   157	            )
   158	
   159	    def forward(self, fx):
   160	        fx = self.attn(self.ln_1(fx)) + fx
   161	        fx = self.mlp(self.ln_2(fx)) + fx
   162	        if self.last_layer:
   163	            return self.mlp2(self.ln_3(fx))
   164	        return fx
   165	
   166	
   167	class Transolver(nn.Module):
   168	    def __init__(self, space_dim=1, n_layers=5, n_hidden=256, dropout=0.0,
   169	                 n_head=8, act="gelu", mlp_ratio=1, fun_dim=1, out_dim=1,
   170	                 slice_num=32, ref=8, unified_pos=False,
   171	                 output_fields: list[str] | None = None,
   172	                 output_dims: list[int] | None = None):
   173	        super().__init__()
   174	        self.ref = ref
   175	        self.unified_pos = unified_pos
   176	        self.output_fields = output_fields or []
   177	        self.output_dims = output_dims or []
   178	
   179	        if self.unified_pos:
   180	            self.preprocess = MLP(fun_dim + ref**3, n_hidden * 2, n_hidden,
   181	                                  n_layers=0, res=False, act=act)
   182	        else:
   183	            self.preprocess = MLP(fun_dim + space_dim, n_hidden * 2, n_hidden,
   184	                                  n_layers=0, res=False, act=act)
   185	
   186	        self.n_hidden = n_hidden
   187	        self.space_dim = space_dim
   188	        self.blocks = nn.ModuleList([
   189	            TransolverBlock(
   190	                num_heads=n_head, hidden_dim=n_hidden, dropout=dropout,
   191	                act=act, mlp_ratio=mlp_ratio, out_dim=out_dim,
   192	                slice_num=slice_num, last_layer=(i == n_layers - 1),
   193	            )
   194	            for i in range(n_layers)
   195	        ])
   196	        self.placeholder = nn.Parameter((1 / n_hidden) * torch.rand(n_hidden))
   197	        self.apply(self._init_weights)
   198	
   199	    def _init_weights(self, m):
   200	        if isinstance(m, nn.Linear):
   201	            trunc_normal_(m.weight, std=0.02)
   202	            if m.bias is not None:
   203	                nn.init.constant_(m.bias, 0)
   204	        elif isinstance(m, (nn.LayerNorm, nn.BatchNorm1d)):
   205	            nn.init.constant_(m.bias, 0)
   206	            nn.init.constant_(m.weight, 1.0)
   207	
   208	    def forward(self, data, **kwargs):
   209	        x = data["x"]
   210	        fx = self.preprocess(x) + self.placeholder[None, None, :]
   211	        for block in self.blocks:
   212	            fx = block(fx)
   213	        return {"preds": fx}
   214	
   215	
   216	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   217	# Evaluation helpers
   218	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   219	
   220	def evaluate_split(model, loader, stats, surf_weight, device) -> dict[str, float]:
   221	    """Run inference over a split and return metrics matching the organizer scorer.
   222	
   223	    ``loss`` is the normalized-space loss used for training monitoring; the MAE
   224	    channels are in the original target space and accumulated per organizer
   225	    ``score.py`` (float64, non-finite samples skipped).
   226	    """
   227	    vol_loss_sum = surf_loss_sum = 0.0
   228	    mae_surf = torch.zeros(3, dtype=torch.float64, device=device)
   229	    mae_vol = torch.zeros(3, dtype=torch.float64, device=device)
   230	    n_surf = n_vol = n_batches = 0
   231	
   232	    with torch.no_grad():
   233	        for x, y, is_surface, mask in loader:
   234	            x = x.to(device, non_blocking=True)
   235	            y = y.to(device, non_blocking=True)
   236	            is_surface = is_surface.to(device, non_blocking=True)
   237	            mask = mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
   238	
   239	            x_norm = (x - stats["x_mean"]) / stats["x_std"]
   240	            y_norm = (y - stats["y_mean"]) / stats["y_std"]
   241	            pred = model({"x": x_norm})["preds"]
   242	
   243	            sq_err = (pred - y_norm) ** 2
   244	            vol_mask = mask & ~is_surface
   245	            surf_mask = mask & is_surface
   246	            vol_loss_sum += (
   247	                (sq_err * vol_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum()
   248	                / vol_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
   249	            ).item()
   250	            surf_loss_sum += (
   251	                (sq_err * surf_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum()
   252	                / surf_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
   253	            ).item()
   254	            n_batches += 1
   255	
   256	            pred_orig = pred * stats["y_std"] + stats["y_mean"]
   257	            ds, dv = accumulate_batch(pred_orig, y, is_surface, mask, mae_surf, mae_vol)
   258	            n_surf += ds
   259	            n_vol += dv
   260	
   261	    vol_loss = vol_loss_sum / max(n_batches, 1)
   262	    surf_loss = surf_loss_sum / max(n_batches, 1)
   263	    out = {"vol_loss": vol_loss, "surf_loss": surf_loss,
   264	           "loss": vol_loss + surf_weight * surf_loss}
   265	    out.update(finalize_split(mae_surf, mae_vol, n_surf, n_vol))
   266	    return out
   267	
   268	
   269	def _sanitize_artifact_token(s: str) -> str:
   270	    """wandb artifact names allow alnum, '-', '_', '.' — replace everything else."""
   271	    out = "".join(c if c.isalnum() or c in "-_." else "-" for c in s)
   272	    return out.strip("-_.") or "run"
   273	
   274	
   275	def _git_commit_short() -> str:
   276	    try:
   277	        return subprocess.check_output(
   278	            ["git", "rev-parse", "--short", "HEAD"],
   279	            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL, text=True,
   280	        ).strip() or "unknown"
   281	    except (subprocess.CalledProcessError, FileNotFoundError):
   282	        return "unknown"
   283	
   284	
   285	def save_model_artifact(
   286	    run,
   287	    model_path: Path,
   288	    model_dir: Path,
   289	    cfg: "Config",
   290	    best_metrics: dict,
   291	    best_avg_surf_p: float,
   292	    test_metrics: dict | None,
   293	    test_avg: dict | None,
   294	    n_params: int,
   295	    model_config: dict,
   296	) -> None:
   297	    """Log the best checkpoint as a wandb model artifact.
   298	
   299	    Name: ``model-<agent-or-wandb_name>-<run.id>`` (run.id guarantees uniqueness).
   300	    Aliases: ``best`` + ``epoch-N`` so the best checkpoint is addressable
   301	    both by role and by the epoch it came from.
   302	    Payload: ``checkpoint.pt`` + ``config.yaml`` (if present).
   303	    Metadata: run context, selected val metric, optional test metrics, git
   304	    commit, model config, and hyperparams — enough to trace and reload.
   305	    """
   306	    if cfg.wandb_name:
   307	        base = _sanitize_artifact_token(cfg.wandb_name)
   308	    elif cfg.agent:
   309	        base = _sanitize_artifact_token(cfg.agent)
   310	    else:
   311	        base = "tandemfoil"
   312	    artifact_name = f"model-{base}-{run.id}"
   313	
   314	    metadata: dict = {
   315	        "run_id": run.id,
   316	        "run_name": run.name,
   317	        "agent": cfg.agent,
   318	        "wandb_name": cfg.wandb_name,
   319	        "wandb_group": cfg.wandb_group,
   320	        "git_commit": _git_commit_short(),
   321	        "n_params": n_params,
   322	        "model_config": model_config,
   323	        "best_epoch": best_metrics["epoch"],
   324	        "best_val_avg/mae_surf_p": best_avg_surf_p,
   325	        "lr": cfg.lr,
   326	        "weight_decay": cfg.weight_decay,
   327	        "batch_size": cfg.batch_size,
   328	        "surf_weight": cfg.surf_weight,
   329	        "epochs_configured": cfg.epochs,
   330	    }
   331	
   332	    description = (
   333	        f"Transolver checkpoint — best val_avg/mae_surf_p = {best_avg_surf_p:.4f} "
   334	        f"at epoch {best_metrics['epoch']}"
   335	    )
   336	
   337	    if test_avg is not None and "avg/mae_surf_p" in test_avg:
   338	        metadata["test_avg/mae_surf_p"] = test_avg["avg/mae_surf_p"]
   339	        if test_metrics is not None:
   340	            for split_name, m in test_metrics.items():
   341	                metadata[f"test/{split_name}/mae_surf_p"] = m["mae_surf_p"]
   342	        description += f" | test_avg/mae_surf_p = {test_avg['avg/mae_surf_p']:.4f}"
   343	
   344	    artifact = wandb.Artifact(
   345	        name=artifact_name,
   346	        type="model",
   347	        description=description,
   348	        metadata=metadata,
   349	    )
   350	    artifact.add_file(str(model_path), name="checkpoint.pt")
   351	    config_yaml = model_dir / "config.yaml"
   352	    if config_yaml.exists():
   353	        artifact.add_file(str(config_yaml), name="config.yaml")
   354	
   355	    aliases = ["best", f"epoch-{best_metrics['epoch']}"]
   356	    run.log_artifact(artifact, aliases=aliases)
   357	    print(f"\nLogged model artifact '{artifact_name}' (aliases: {', '.join(aliases)})")
   358	
   359	
   360	def print_split_metrics(split_name: str, m: dict[str, float]) -> None:
   361	    print(
   362	        f"    {split_name:<26s} "
   363	        f"loss={m['loss']:.4f}  "
   364	        f"surf[p={m['mae_surf_p']:.4f} Ux={m['mae_surf_Ux']:.4f} Uy={m['mae_surf_Uy']:.4f}]  "
   365	        f"vol[p={m['mae_vol_p']:.4f} Ux={m['mae_vol_Ux']:.4f} Uy={m['mae_vol_Uy']:.4f}]"
   366	    )
   367	
   368	
   369	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   370	# Training
   371	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   372	
   373	DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MIN = float(os.environ.get("SENPAI_TIMEOUT_MINUTES", "30"))
   374	
   375	
   376	@dataclass
   377	class Config:
   378	    lr: float = 5e-4
   379	    weight_decay: float = 1e-4
   380	    batch_size: int = 4
   381	    surf_weight: float = 10.0
   382	    epochs: int = 50
   383	    splits_dir: str = "/mnt/new-pvc/datasets/tandemfoil/splits_v2"
   384	    wandb_group: str | None = None
   385	    wandb_name: str | None = None
   386	    agent: str | None = None
   387	    debug: bool = False
   388	    skip_test: bool = False  # skip end-of-run test evaluation
   389	
   390	
   391	cfg = sp.parse(Config)
   392	MAX_EPOCHS = 3 if cfg.debug else cfg.epochs
   393	MAX_TIMEOUT_MIN = DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MIN
   394	
   395	device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
   396	print(f"Device: {device}" + (" [DEBUG]" if cfg.debug else ""))
   397	
   398	train_ds, val_splits, stats, sample_weights = load_data(cfg.splits_dir, debug=cfg.debug)
   399	stats = {k: v.to(device) for k, v in stats.items()}
   400	
   401	loader_kwargs = dict(collate_fn=pad_collate, num_workers=4, pin_memory=True,
   402	                     persistent_workers=True, prefetch_factor=2)
   403	
   404	if cfg.debug:
   405	    train_loader = DataLoader(train_ds, batch_size=cfg.batch_size,
   406	                              shuffle=True, **loader_kwargs)
   407	else:
   408	    sampler = WeightedRandomSampler(sample_weights, num_samples=len(train_ds), replacement=True)
   409	    train_loader = DataLoader(train_ds, batch_size=cfg.batch_size,
   410	                              sampler=sampler, **loader_kwargs)
   411	
   412	val_loaders = {
   413	    name: DataLoader(ds, batch_size=cfg.batch_size, shuffle=False, **loader_kwargs)
   414	    for name, ds in val_splits.items()
   415	}
   416	
   417	model_config = dict(
   418	    space_dim=2,
   419	    fun_dim=X_DIM - 2,
   420	    out_dim=3,
   421	    n_hidden=128,
   422	    n_layers=5,
   423	    n_head=4,
   424	    slice_num=64,
   425	    mlp_ratio=2,
   426	    output_fields=["Ux", "Uy", "p"],
   427	    output_dims=[1, 1, 1],
   428	)
   429	
   430	model = Transolver(**model_config).to(device)
   431	n_params = sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters())
   432	print(f"Model: Transolver ({n_params/1e6:.2f}M params)")
   433	
   434	optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=cfg.lr, weight_decay=cfg.weight_decay)
   435	scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=MAX_EPOCHS)
   436	
   437	run = wandb.init(
   438	    entity=os.environ.get("WANDB_ENTITY"),
   439	    project=os.environ.get("WANDB_PROJECT"),
   440	    group=cfg.wandb_group,
   441	    name=cfg.wandb_name,
   442	    tags=[cfg.agent] if cfg.agent else [],
   443	    config={
   444	        **asdict(cfg),
   445	        "model_config": model_config,
   446	        "n_params": n_params,
   447	        "train_samples": len(train_ds),
   448	        "val_samples": {k: len(v) for k, v in val_splits.items()},
   449	    },
   450	    mode=os.environ.get("WANDB_MODE", "online"),
   451	)
   452	
   453	wandb.define_metric("global_step")
   454	wandb.define_metric("train/*", step_metric="global_step")
   455	wandb.define_metric("val/*", step_metric="global_step")
   456	for _name in VAL_SPLIT_NAMES:
   457	    wandb.define_metric(f"{_name}/*", step_metric="global_step")
   458	wandb.define_metric("lr", step_metric="global_step")
   459	
   460	model_dir = Path(f"models/model-{run.id}")
   461	model_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   462	model_path = model_dir / "checkpoint.pt"
   463	with open(model_dir / "config.yaml", "w") as f:
   464	    yaml.dump(model_config, f)
   465	
   466	best_avg_surf_p = float("inf")
   467	best_metrics: dict = {}
   468	global_step = 0
   469	train_start = time.time()
   470	
   471	for epoch in range(MAX_EPOCHS):
   472	    if (time.time() - train_start) / 60.0 >= MAX_TIMEOUT_MIN:
   473	        print(f"Timeout ({MAX_TIMEOUT_MIN} min). Stopping.")
   474	        break
   475	
   476	    t0 = time.time()
   477	    model.train()
   478	    epoch_vol = epoch_surf = 0.0
   479	    n_batches = 0
   480	
   481	    for x, y, is_surface, mask in tqdm(train_loader, desc=f"Epoch {epoch+1}/{MAX_EPOCHS}", leave=False):
   482	        x = x.to(device, non_blocking=True)
   483	        y = y.to(device, non_blocking=True)
   484	        is_surface = is_surface.to(device, non_blocking=True)
   485	        mask = mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
   486	
   487	        x_norm = (x - stats["x_mean"]) / stats["x_std"]
   488	        y_norm = (y - stats["y_mean"]) / stats["y_std"]
   489	        pred = model({"x": x_norm})["preds"]
   490	        sq_err = (pred - y_norm) ** 2
   491	
   492	        vol_mask = mask & ~is_surface
   493	        surf_mask = mask & is_surface
   494	        vol_loss = (sq_err * vol_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum() / vol_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
   495	        surf_loss = (sq_err * surf_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum() / surf_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
   496	        loss = vol_loss + cfg.surf_weight * surf_loss
   497	
   498	        optimizer.zero_grad()
   499	        loss.backward()
   500	        optimizer.step()
   501	        global_step += 1
   502	        wandb.log({"train/loss": loss.item(), "global_step": global_step})
   503	
   504	        epoch_vol += vol_loss.item()
   505	        epoch_surf += surf_loss.item()
   506	        n_batches += 1
   507	
   508	    scheduler.step()
   509	    epoch_vol /= max(n_batches, 1)
   510	    epoch_surf /= max(n_batches, 1)
   511	
   512	    # --- Validate ---
   513	    model.eval()
   514	    split_metrics = {
   515	        name: evaluate_split(model, loader, stats, cfg.surf_weight, device)
   516	        for name, loader in val_loaders.items()
   517	    }
   518	    val_avg = aggregate_splits(split_metrics)
   519	    avg_surf_p = val_avg["avg/mae_surf_p"]
   520	    val_loss_mean = sum(m["loss"] for m in split_metrics.values()) / len(split_metrics)
   521	    dt = time.time() - t0
   522	
   523	    log_metrics = {
   524	        "train/vol_loss": epoch_vol,
   525	        "train/surf_loss": epoch_surf,
   526	        "val/loss": val_loss_mean,
   527	        "lr": scheduler.get_last_lr()[0],
   528	        "epoch_time_s": dt,
   529	        "global_step": global_step,
   530	    }
   531	    for split_name, m in split_metrics.items():
   532	        for k, v in m.items():
   533	            log_metrics[f"{split_name}/{k}"] = v
   534	    for k, v in val_avg.items():
   535	        log_metrics[f"val_{k}"] = v  # val_avg/mae_surf_p etc.
   536	    wandb.log(log_metrics)
   537	
   538	    tag = ""
   539	    if avg_surf_p < best_avg_surf_p:
   540	        best_avg_surf_p = avg_surf_p
   541	        best_metrics = {
   542	            "epoch": epoch + 1,
   543	            "val_avg/mae_surf_p": avg_surf_p,
   544	            "per_split": split_metrics,
   545	        }
   546	        torch.save(model.state_dict(), model_path)
   547	        tag = " *"
   548	
   549	    peak_gb = torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated() / 1e9 if torch.cuda.is_available() else 0.0
   550	    print(
   551	        f"Epoch {epoch+1:3d} ({dt:.0f}s) [{peak_gb:.1f}GB]  "
   552	        f"train[vol={epoch_vol:.4f} surf={epoch_surf:.4f}]  "
   553	        f"val_avg_surf_p={avg_surf_p:.4f}{tag}"
   554	    )
   555	    for name in VAL_SPLIT_NAMES:
   556	        print_split_metrics(name, split_metrics[name])
   557	
   558	total_time = (time.time() - train_start) / 60.0
   559	print(f"\nTraining done in {total_time:.1f} min")
   560	
   561	# --- Test evaluation + artifact upload ---
   562	if best_metrics:
   563	    print(f"\nBest val: epoch {best_metrics['epoch']}, val_avg/mae_surf_p = {best_avg_surf_p:.4f}")
   564	    wandb.summary.update({
   565	        "best_epoch": best_metrics["epoch"],
   566	        "best_val_avg/mae_surf_p": best_avg_surf_p,
   567	        "total_train_minutes": total_time,
   568	    })
   569	
   570	    model.load_state_dict(torch.load(model_path, map_location=device, weights_only=True))
   571	    model.eval()
   572	
   573	    test_metrics = None
   574	    test_avg = None
   575	    if not cfg.skip_test:
   576	        print("\nEvaluating on held-out test splits...")
   577	        test_datasets = load_test_data(cfg.splits_dir, debug=cfg.debug)
   578	        test_loaders = {
   579	            name: DataLoader(ds, batch_size=cfg.batch_size, shuffle=False, **loader_kwargs)
   580	            for name, ds in test_datasets.items()
   581	        }
   582	        test_metrics = {
   583	            name: evaluate_split(model, loader, stats, cfg.surf_weight, device)
   584	            for name, loader in test_loaders.items()
   585	        }
   586	        test_avg = aggregate_splits(test_metrics)
   587	        print(f"\n  TEST  avg_surf_p={test_avg['avg/mae_surf_p']:.4f}")
   588	        for name in TEST_SPLIT_NAMES:
   589	            print_split_metrics(name, test_metrics[name])
   590	
   591	        test_log: dict[str, float] = {}
   592	        for split_name, m in test_metrics.items():
   593	            for k, v in m.items():
   594	                test_log[f"test/{split_name}/{k}"] = v
   595	        for k, v in test_avg.items():
   596	            test_log[f"test_{k}"] = v
   597	        wandb.log(test_log)
   598	        wandb.summary.update(test_log)
   599	
   600	    save_model_artifact(
   601	        run=run,
   602	        model_path=model_path,
   603	        model_dir=model_dir,
   604	        cfg=cfg,
   605	        best_metrics=best_metrics,
   606	        best_avg_surf_p=best_avg_surf_p,
   607	        test_metrics=test_metrics,
   608	        test_avg=test_avg,
   609	        n_params=n_params,
   610	        model_config=model_config,
   611	    )
   612	else:
   613	    print("\nNo checkpoint was saved (no epoch improved on val_avg/mae_surf_p). Skipping artifact upload.")
   614	
   615	wandb.finish()

messages.content:
1	"""Dataset and loaders for the pre-materialized TandemFoil splits on PVC.
     2	
     3	Run ``python data/prepare_splits.py`` once on the PVC to materialize
     4	``splits_v2/`` from ``data/split_manifest.json``; this module then streams
     5	per-sample ``.pt`` files on demand.
     6	"""
     7	
     8	import json
     9	from pathlib import Path
    10	
    11	import torch
    12	from torch.utils.data import Dataset
    13	
    14	X_DIM = 24
    15	
    16	VAL_SPLIT_NAMES = [
    17	    "val_single_in_dist",
    18	    "val_geom_camber_rc",
    19	    "val_geom_camber_cruise",
    20	    "val_re_rand",
    21	]
    22	
    23	TEST_SPLIT_NAMES = [
    24	    "test_single_in_dist",
    25	    "test_geom_camber_rc",
    26	    "test_geom_camber_cruise",
    27	    "test_re_rand",
    28	]
    29	
    30	SPLITS_DIR = Path("/mnt/new-pvc/datasets/tandemfoil/splits_v2")
    31	
    32	
    33	class SplitDataset(Dataset):
    34	    """Dataset backed by individual ``{x, y, is_surface}`` .pt files."""
    35	
    36	    def __init__(self, directory: str | Path):
    37	        self.directory = Path(directory)
    38	        self.files = sorted(self.directory.glob("*.pt"))
    39	
    40	    def __len__(self):
    41	        return len(self.files)
    42	
    43	    def __getitem__(self, idx):
    44	        s = torch.load(self.files[idx], weights_only=True)
    45	        return s["x"], s["y"], s["is_surface"]
    46	
    47	
    48	class TestDataset(Dataset):
    49	    """Test split dataset: reads ``x`` from ``test_*/`` and ``y`` from ``.test_*_gt/``.
    50	
    51	    Ground truth is held separately on PVC so prediction submissions can be
    52	    scored blind. For in-repo training, we load both sides here.
    53	    """
    54	
    55	    def __init__(self, x_dir: str | Path, gt_dir: str | Path):
    56	        self.x_files = sorted(Path(x_dir).glob("*.pt"))
    57	        self.gt_files = sorted(Path(gt_dir).glob("*.pt"))
    58	        assert len(self.x_files) == len(self.gt_files), (
    59	            f"Test split file-count mismatch: {len(self.x_files)} x-files vs "
    60	            f"{len(self.gt_files)} gt-files"
    61	        )
    62	
    63	    def __len__(self):
    64	        return len(self.x_files)
    65	
    66	    def __getitem__(self, idx):
    67	        xs = torch.load(self.x_files[idx], weights_only=True)
    68	        gt = torch.load(self.gt_files[idx], weights_only=True)
    69	        return xs["x"], gt["y"], gt["is_surface"]
    70	
    71	
    72	def pad_collate(batch):
    73	    """Pad variable-length mesh samples into a batch.
    74	
    75	    Returns (x, y, is_surface, mask), each ``[B, N_max, ...]``.
    76	    """
    77	    xs, ys, surfs = zip(*batch)
    78	    max_n = max(x.shape[0] for x in xs)
    79	    B = len(xs)
    80	    x_pad = torch.zeros(B, max_n, xs[0].shape[1])
    81	    y_pad = torch.zeros(B, max_n, ys[0].shape[1])
    82	    surf_pad = torch.zeros(B, max_n, dtype=torch.bool)
    83	    mask = torch.zeros(B, max_n, dtype=torch.bool)
    84	    for i, (x, y, sf) in enumerate(zip(xs, ys, surfs)):
    85	        n = x.shape[0]
    86	        x_pad[i, :n] = x
    87	        y_pad[i, :n] = y
    88	        surf_pad[i, :n] = sf
    89	        mask[i, :n] = True
    90	    return x_pad, y_pad, surf_pad, mask
    91	
    92	
    93	def _load_stats(splits_dir: Path) -> dict[str, torch.Tensor]:
    94	    with open(splits_dir / "stats.json") as f:
    95	        raw = json.load(f)
    96	    return {k: torch.tensor(raw[k], dtype=torch.float32) for k in ("x_mean", "x_std", "y_mean", "y_std")}
    97	
    98	
    99	def load_data(
   100	    splits_dir: str | Path = SPLITS_DIR,
   101	    debug: bool = False,
   102	) -> tuple[SplitDataset, dict[str, SplitDataset], dict[str, torch.Tensor], torch.Tensor]:
   103	    """Train + val datasets, normalization stats, and balanced-domain weights.
   104	
   105	    Returns ``(train_ds, val_splits, stats, sample_weights)``.
   106	    """
   107	    splits_dir = Path(splits_dir)
   108	
   109	    with open(splits_dir / "meta.json") as f:
   110	        meta = json.load(f)
   111	    stats = _load_stats(splits_dir)
   112	
   113	    train_ds = SplitDataset(splits_dir / "train")
   114	    val_splits = {name: SplitDataset(splits_dir / name) for name in VAL_SPLIT_NAMES}
   115	
   116	    if debug:
   117	        train_ds.files = train_ds.files[:6]
   118	        for ds in val_splits.values():
   119	            ds.files = ds.files[:2]
   120	
   121	    domain_groups = meta["domain_groups"]
   122	    group_sizes = {name: len(idxs) for name, idxs in domain_groups.items()}
   123	    idx_to_group: dict[int, str] = {}
   124	    for name, idxs in domain_groups.items():
   125	        for i in idxs:
   126	            idx_to_group[i] = name
   127	
   128	    sample_weights = torch.tensor(
   129	        [1.0 / group_sizes[idx_to_group[i]] for i in range(len(train_ds))],
   130	        dtype=torch.float64,
   131	    )
   132	
   133	    print(
   134	        f"Train: {len(train_ds)}, "
   135	        + ", ".join(f"{k}: {len(v)}" for k, v in val_splits.items())
   136	    )
   137	    return train_ds, val_splits, stats, sample_weights
   138	
   139	
   140	def load_test_data(
   141	    splits_dir: str | Path = SPLITS_DIR,
   142	    debug: bool = False,
   143	) -> dict[str, TestDataset]:
   144	    """Test datasets keyed by split name (with joined hidden ground truth)."""
   145	    splits_dir = Path(splits_dir)
   146	    test_splits: dict[str, TestDataset] = {}
   147	    for name in TEST_SPLIT_NAMES:
   148	        ds = TestDataset(splits_dir / name, splits_dir / f".{name}_gt")
   149	        if debug:
   150	            ds.x_files = ds.x_files[:2]
   151	            ds.gt_files = ds.gt_files[:2]
   152	        test_splits[name] = ds
   153	    print("Test: " + ", ".join(f"{k}: {len(v)}" for k, v in test_splits.items()))
   154	    return test_splits

messages.content:
1	"""Split MAE accumulation matching ``data/score_split`` semantics.
     2	
     3	Semantics pinned to what the organizer scoring script (``score.py``) computes
     4	against the hidden test ground truth:
     5	
     6	- float64 accumulation for numerical stability over many samples
     7	- per-sample skip for any case whose ground truth contains non-finite values
     8	- global (node-level) aggregation: surface MAE is ``sum|err| / n_surf_nodes``,
     9	  not a per-sample average
    10	
    11	The same helpers are used for validation during training and for the end-of-run
    12	test evaluation, so train/val/test metrics are computed identically.
    13	"""
    14	
    15	from __future__ import annotations
    16	
    17	import torch
    18	
    19	CHANNELS = ("Ux", "Uy", "p")
    20	
    21	
    22	def accumulate_batch(
    23	    pred_orig: torch.Tensor,
    24	    y: torch.Tensor,
    25	    is_surface: torch.Tensor,
    26	    mask: torch.Tensor,
    27	    mae_surf: torch.Tensor,
    28	    mae_vol: torch.Tensor,
    29	) -> tuple[int, int]:
    30	    """Add a padded batch's surface/volume MAE contribution into the accumulators.
    31	
    32	    ``pred_orig`` and ``y`` are in the original (denormalized) output space.
    33	    Shapes: all are ``[B, N_max, ...]``; ``mask`` marks valid nodes.
    34	    Samples whose ground-truth is non-finite anywhere are skipped entirely.
    35	
    36	    Returns ``(n_surf_added, n_vol_added)``.
    37	    """
    38	    B = y.shape[0]
    39	    y_finite = torch.isfinite(y.reshape(B, -1)).all(dim=-1)  # [B]
    40	    if not y_finite.any():
    41	        return 0, 0
    42	
    43	    sample_mask = y_finite.unsqueeze(-1).expand(-1, mask.shape[-1])  # [B, N]
    44	    effective = mask & sample_mask
    45	    surf_mask = effective & is_surface
    46	    vol_mask = effective & ~is_surface
    47	
    48	    err = (pred_orig.double() - y.double()).abs()
    49	    mae_surf += (err * surf_mask.unsqueeze(-1).double()).sum(dim=(0, 1))
    50	    mae_vol += (err * vol_mask.unsqueeze(-1).double()).sum(dim=(0, 1))
    51	    return int(surf_mask.sum().item()), int(vol_mask.sum().item())
    52	
    53	
    54	def finalize_split(
    55	    mae_surf: torch.Tensor,
    56	    mae_vol: torch.Tensor,
    57	    n_surf: int,
    58	    n_vol: int,
    59	) -> dict[str, float]:
    60	    """Divide accumulators by node counts to get per-channel MAE."""
    61	    s = mae_surf / max(n_surf, 1)
    62	    v = mae_vol / max(n_vol, 1)
    63	    out: dict[str, float] = {}
    64	    for i, ch in enumerate(CHANNELS):
    65	        out[f"mae_surf_{ch}"] = s[i].item()
    66	        out[f"mae_vol_{ch}"] = v[i].item()
    67	    return out
    68	
    69	
    70	def aggregate_splits(per_split: dict[str, dict[str, float]]) -> dict[str, float]:
    71	    """Equal-weight mean across splits of each per-channel MAE.
    72	
    73	    Primary ranking metric: ``avg/mae_surf_p`` (surface pressure MAE
    74	    averaged with equal weight across the four split tracks).
    75	    """
    76	    out: dict[str, float] = {}
    77	    keys = [f"mae_{loc}_{ch}" for loc in ("surf", "vol") for ch in CHANNELS]
    78	    for k in keys:
    79	        vals = [m[k] for m in per_split.values() if k in m]
    80	        if vals:
    81	            out[f"avg/{k}"] = sum(vals) / len(vals)
    82	    return out

1	# TandemFoilSet-Balanced
     2	
     3	This repo contains:
     4	1) a custom TandemFoilSet dataset split called `TandemFoilSet-Balanced` based on the **[TandemFoilSet Paper](https://openreview.net/forum?id=4Z0P4Nbosn)**
     5	2) a base set of data and training files that the [senpai](https://github.com/wandb/senpai) autoresearch harness can use as a baseline to try and improve performance on this dataset.
     6	
     7	## Citation
     8	
     9	If you use this balanced split design or benchmark package, please cite the **BibTeX for TandemFoilSet-Balanced**
    10	
    11	```bibtex
    12	@misc{mcguire2026tandemfoilsetbalanced,
    13	  title        = {{TandemFoilSet-Balanced}: Balanced Split Design and CFD Surrogate Benchmark Package},
    14	  author       = {McGuire, Morgan and Capelle, Thomas},
    15	  year         = {2026},
    16	  howpublished = {GitHub repository},
    17	  url          = {https://github.com/morganmcg1/TandemFoilSet-Balanced},
    18	  note         = {Accessed 2026-04-24}
    19	}
    20	```
    21	
    22	## TandemFoilSet-Balanced Splits Rationale
    23	The goal behind this split is to be able to say:
    24	- The trained model is good/bad at generalizing to unseen geometries (train on low + high camber, val/test on moderate)
    25	- AND the same model works across different Reynolds numbers for both race car and cruise (train on low, med, high Re, val/test on low, moderate, high) - random split across all Re numbers
    26	- AND the model performs well on a single-foil random split as a sanity check
    27	
    28	See **[SPLITS.md](data/SPLITS.md)** for a full description of the dataset splits.
    29	
    30	## Training Files
    31	
    32	Below are details of the training files that can be used in an autoresearch context. These provide an agent with a base train.py file as well as instructions of metrics to optimize, information about the dataset and file to preprocess the data and calculate results.
    33	
    34	### Files Layout
    35	
    36	```
    37	.
    38	├── README.md
    39	├── program.md                 # research contract — read this first
    40	├── train.py                   # Transolver trainer; primary editable entrypoint
    41	├── instructions/
    42	│   ├── prompt-advisor.md
    43	│   └── prompt-student.md
    44	└── data/
    45	    ├── __init__.py
    46	    ├── loader.py              # SplitDataset, TestDataset, load_data, load_test_data
    47	    ├── scoring.py             # MAE accumulation shared by val and test
    48	    ├── prepare_splits.py      # one-shot: materialize .pt samples onto the PVC
    49	    ├── generate_manifest.py   # regenerate split_manifest.json from raw data
    50	    ├── split_manifest.json    # pinned split definition (source of truth)
    51	    └── SPLITS.md              # split design, holdout reasons, per-file counts
    52	```
    53	
    54	### Data
    55	
    56	Pre-processed samples live on the PVC at `/mnt/new-pvc/datasets/tandemfoil/splits_v2/` (private author-specific PVC, you'll have to download the dataset yourself to be able to run this code). Materialize them once from the manifest:
    57	
    58	```
    59	python data/prepare_splits.py
    60	```
    61	
    62	After that, `train.py` streams `.pt` samples directly — no re-preprocessing per run.
    63	
    64	See `program.md` for input feature layout (24 input dims), target channels, split design, and the full metric contract.
    65	
    66	### Training
    67	
    68	```
    69	python train.py [--debug] [--epochs 50] [--agent <name>] [--wandb_name <name>]
    70	```
    71	
    72	The trainer logs per-epoch metrics to W&B and prints a per-split MAE breakdown to the console every epoch. At the end of the run it loads the best checkpoint (selected on `val_avg/mae_surf_p`) and evaluates it on the four held-out test splits, logging `test_avg/mae_surf_p` plus per-split per-channel MAEs.
    73	
    74	Environment:
    75	
    76	- `SENPAI_TIMEOUT_MINUTES` — wall-clock cap (default 30)
    77	- `WANDB_ENTITY`, `WANDB_PROJECT`, `WANDB_MODE` — W&B routing
    78	
    79	## Investigation: SENPAI Students vs KAgent Competition
    80	
    81	Results from the 12-hour [KAgent](https://github.com/tcapelle/kagent) vs [senpai](https://github.com/wandb/senpai) autoresearch head-to-head on 2026-04-24. Senpai results are documented here and tracked in the [W&B project](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/senpai-kagent-v-students). Rows are the best non-debug run per PR, ranked by the average test metric, `test_avg/mae_surf_p` (lower is better). Split columns are test-set surface-pressure MAE. The KAgent results for this competition can be found here: 
    82	
    83	Note: wandb run accounting uses W&B `state=finished`, not finite test score. The competition produced 544 total W&B runs: 450 finished, 77 crashed, and 17 failed. Of the 450 finished runs, 360 had finite `test_avg/mae_surf_p` and 90 finished without a finite `test_avg/mae_surf_p`. GitHub has 41 actual PRs in the competition range (`#1`-`#43`, with no `#10` or `#13`); excluding debug/smoke-check runs gives 417 completed non-debug W&B runs.

   123	### BibTeX for the original TandemFoilSet paper
   124	
   125	For the underlying dataset, cite the original TandemFoilSet paper:
   126	
   127	```bibtex
   128	@inproceedings{lim2026tandemfoilset,
   129	  title     = {{TandemFoilSet}: Datasets for Flow Field Prediction of Tandem-Airfoil Through the Reuse of Single Airfoils},

[project]
name = "tandemfoil2"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Transolver surrogate trainer for TandemFoilSet (velocity + pressure fields)"
authors = [
    { name = "Morgan McGuire" },
    { name = "Thomas Capelle" },
]
readme = "README.md"
requires-python = ">=3.10"
dependencies = [
    "numpy",
    "torch",
    "einops",
    "timm",
    "simple-parsing",
    "tqdm",
    "rich",
    "wandb",
    "pyyaml",
]

[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=68"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"

[tool.setuptools]
py-modules = ["train"]

[tool.setuptools.packages.find]
include = ["data*"]
