            x_norm = (x - stats["x_mean"]) / stats["x_std"]
            y_norm = (y - stats["y_mean"]) / stats["y_std"]", "new_str": "        for x, y, is_surface, mask in tqdm(
            train_loader, desc=f"Epoch {epoch+1}/{MAX_EPOCHS}", leave=False, disable=True,
        ):
            x = x.to(device, non_blocking=True)
            y = y.to(device, non_blocking=True)
            is_surface = is_surface.to(device, non_blocking=True)
            mask = mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)

            if cfg.subsample > 0:
                x, y, is_surface, mask = subsample_batch(
                    x, y, is_surface, mask, cfg.subsample, cfg.surf_oversample
                )

            x_norm = (x - stats["x_mean"]) / stats["x_std"]
            y_norm = (y - stats["y_mean"]) / stats["y_std"]"}

function.arguments:
{"path": "/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/scripts/collect_results.py", "content": """"Collect training run results from session_logs/*.log into MLINTERN_RESULTS.jsonl. Each line is a JSON object with: run_name, log_file, status (running|finished|crashed|timeout), best_val_avg_mae_surf_p, best_epoch, last_epoch_metrics{}, test_avg_metrics{}, per_split_test{}, n_params, total_train_minutes, peak_gb, lr, weight_decay, ... extracted hyperparameters from config. """ import argparse import json import re import sys from pathlib import Path # Anchored summary fields the trainer prints near the end of a run. EPOCH_RE = re.compile( r"^Epoch\s+(?P\d+)\s+\((?P
\d+)s\)\s+\[(?P[\d.]+)GB\]\s+" r"train\[vol=(?P[\d.naN-]+)\s+surf=(?P[\d.naN-]+)\]\s+" r"val_avg_surf_p=(?P[\d.naN-]+)(?P\s\*)?\s+lr=(?P[\d.e+-]+)" ) SPLIT_RE = re.compile( r"^\s+(?P\S+)\s+loss=(?P[\d.naN-]+)\s+" r"surf\[p=(?P[\d.naN-]+)\s+Ux=(?P[\d.naN-]+)\s+Uy=(?P[\d.naN-]+)\]\s+" r"vol\[p=(?P[\d.naN-]+)\s+Ux=(?P[\d.naN-]+)\s+Uy=(?P[\d.naN-]+)\]" ) TEST_AVG_RE = re.compile(r"TEST\s+avg_surf_p=(?P[\d.naN-]+)") BEST_VAL_RE = re.compile(r"Best val: epoch (?P\d+),\s+val_avg/mae_surf_p\s*=\s*(?P[\d.naN-]+)") TOTAL_TIME_RE = re.compile(r"Training done in (?P[\d.]+) min") MODEL_PARAMS_RE = re.compile(r"Model: Transolver \((?P

[\d.]+)M params\)") WANDB_RUN_RE = re.compile(r"View run at https?://wandb.ai/[^/]+/[^/]+/runs/(?P\w+)") WANDB_NAME_RE = re.compile(r"Syncing run\s+(?P\S+)") def parse_log(log: Path) -> dict: text = log.read_text(errors="ignore") lines = text.splitlines() info: dict = {"log_file": str(log), "run_name": log.stem} # Extract from CLI arguments line if present (it's in the wandb config) # Or just parse our printed messages. # CLI args from filename pattern wave?-gpu?-NAME m = re.match(r"^wave[a-z0-9]+-gpu(\d+)-(.+)$", log.stem) if m: info["gpu_idx"] = int(m.group(1)) info["short_name"] = m.group(2) if (m := MODEL_PARAMS_RE.search(text)): info["n_params_m"] = float(m.group("p")) if (m := WANDB_RUN_RE.search(text)): info["wandb_run_id"] = m.group("rid") if (m := WANDB_NAME_RE.search(text)): info["wandb_run_name"] = m.group("n") # All epoch lines epochs: list[dict] = [] for line in lines: m = EPOCH_RE.match(line) if m: ep = int(m.group("epoch")) d = m.groupdict() epochs.append({ "epoch": ep, "epoch_time_s": int(d["dt"]), "peak_gb": float(d["gb"]), "train_vol_loss": _f(d["train_vol"]), "train_surf_loss": _f(d["train_surf"]), "val_avg_mae_surf_p": _f(d["vasp"]), "is_best": d["best"] is not None, "lr": _f(d["lr"]), }) info["n_epochs_completed"] = len(epochs) info["epochs"] = epochs # Best val best = None for e in epochs: v = e["val_avg_mae_surf_p"] if v is not None and (best is None or v < best["val_avg_mae_surf_p"]): best = {"epoch": e["epoch"], "val_avg_mae_surf_p": v, "lr": e["lr"]} if (m := BEST_VAL_RE.search(text)): # printed at end of run if completed ep = int(m.group("epoch")) v = _f(m.group("v")) if best is None or v != best.get("val_avg_mae_surf_p"): best = {"epoch": ep, "val_avg_mae_surf_p": v} info["best"] = best if (m := TOTAL_TIME_RE.search(text)): info["total_train_minutes"] = float(m.group("m")) # Test results (only present if run completed test eval) if (m := TEST_AVG_RE.search(text)): info["test_avg_mae_surf_p"] = _f(m.group("tasp")) # Find per-split test lines after that idx = text.index(m.group(0)) block = text[idx:idx + 2000] per_split = {} for line in block.splitlines(): sm = SPLIT_RE.match(line) if sm: d = sm.groupdict() per_split[d["split"]] = { "loss": _f(d["loss"]), "mae_surf_p": _f(d["sp"]), "mae_surf_Ux": _f(d["sUx"]), "mae_surf_Uy": _f(d["sUy"]), "mae_vol_p": _f(d["vp"]), "mae_vol_Ux": _f(d["vUx"]), "mae_vol_Uy": _f(d["vUy"]), } info["test_per_split"] = per_split # Heuristic status if "Logged model artifact" in text: info["status"] = "finished" elif "out of memory" in text.lower() or "OutOfMemoryError" in text: info["status"] = "oom" elif "Traceback" in text: info["status"] = "crashed" elif "Timeout" in text: info["status"] = "timeout" else: info["status"] = "running" return info def _f(s: str | None): if s is None: return None s = s.strip() if s in {"nan", "NaN", ""}: return None try: return float(s) except ValueError: return None def main(): p = argparse.ArgumentParser() p.add_argument("--logs_dir", default="session_logs") p.add_argument("--out", default="research/MLINTERN_RESULTS.jsonl") p.add_argument("--include_pattern", default="wave*.log") args = p.parse_args() logs = sorted(Path(args.logs_dir).glob(args.include_pattern)) out = Path(args.out) out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) rows: list[dict] = [] for log in logs: try: info = parse_log(log) rows.append(info) except Exception as e: print(f"warn: failed to parse {log}: {e}", file=sys.stderr) with out.open("w") as f: for r in rows: f.write(json.dumps(r, default=str) + "\n") print(f"Wrote {len(rows)} rows to {out}") # Print summary print("\nRun summary:") print(f"{'Run':50s} {'Status':10s} {'Best val_avg/mae_surf_p':>22s} {'Test avg':>9s} {'Epochs':>6s}") rows.sort(key=lambda r: r.get("best", {}).get("val_avg_mae_surf_p") or 1e18) for r in rows: best = r.get("best") or {} bvp = best.get("val_avg_mae_surf_p") bvp_s = f"{bvp:.3f}" if bvp is not None else "—" ep = best.get("epoch") or 0 tasp = r.get("test_avg_mae_surf_p") tasp_s = f"{tasp:.3f}" if tasp is not None else "—" n_ep = r.get("n_epochs_completed") or 0 name = r.get("short_name", r["run_name"])[:50] print(f"{name:50s} {r['status']:10s} {bvp_s:>14s} ep{ep:<3d} {tasp_s:>9s} {n_ep:>6d}") if __name__ == "__main__": main() "}

function.arguments:
{"command": "cd /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target && CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 timeout 90 python -c "
import torch, time
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '.')
from train import Transolver, subsample_batch
from data import load_data, pad_collate
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, WeightedRandomSampler

# Test: bigger model with subsampling 32K
device = 'cuda'
m = Transolver(
    space_dim=2, fun_dim=22, out_dim=3,
    n_hidden=256, n_layers=10, n_head=8, slice_num=64, mlp_ratio=4,
    use_ada_temp=True, use_rep_slice=True,
    fourier_freq=16, fourier_scale=5.0,
).to(device)
n = sum(p.numel() for p in m.parameters())
print(f'Bigger model params: {n/1e6:.2f}M')

train_ds, val_splits, stats, sw = load_data()
stats = {k: v.to(device) for k, v in stats.items()}
sampler = WeightedRandomSampler(sw, num_samples=8, replacement=True)
loader = DataLoader(train_ds, batch_size=4, sampler=sampler, collate_fn=pad_collate, num_workers=2)

torch.cuda.empty_cache()
m.train()
opt = torch.optim.AdamW(m.parameters(), lr=1e-4)

for i, batch in enumerate(loader):
    x, y, surf, mask = [t.to(device) for t in batch]
    t0 = time.time()
    x_s, y_s, surf_s, mask_s = subsample_batch(x, y, surf, mask, n_sub=32000, surf_oversample=0.5)
    x_norm = (x_s - stats['x_mean']) / stats['x_std']
    y_norm = (y_s - stats['y_mean']) / stats['y_std']
    pred = m({'x': x_norm}, mask=mask_s)['preds']
    loss = ((pred - y_norm)**2 * mask_s.unsqueeze(-1)).mean()
    opt.zero_grad()
    loss.backward()
    opt.step()
    torch.cuda.synchronize()
    print(f'iter {i} time={time.time()-t0:.2f}s, peak_gb={torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated()/1e9:.1f}, loss={loss.item():.4f}')
    if i >= 2: break
" 2>&1 | tail -10", "description": "Test bigger model + subsample 32K"}

function.arguments:
{"path": "/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/scripts/wave2.sh", "content": "#!/bin/bash
# Wave 2: bigger Transolver via training-time subsampling + Fourier PE.
#
# Logic:
#   - Subsampling 32K nodes per sample (vs full mesh ~140K-240K) reduces
#     activation memory ~7x. This lets us run n_hidden=256, n_layers=10,
#     mlp_ratio=4 comfortably with batch_size=4.
#   - Eval still runs on the full mesh so the metric is identical.
#   - Surface oversampling (50% of subsample budget) ensures plenty of
#     surface gradient signal even with random subsampling.
#
# Each run: 50 epochs. With subsampling, each epoch should be ~50% of the
# baseline epoch time, so ~70 min per run, leaving plenty of buffer.
set -e
cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."
mkdir -p session_logs

GROUP="mlintern-pai2-24h-v2-r1"
AGENT="ml-intern-r1"
EPOCHS=50

start_run() {
  local gpu="$1"; shift
  local name="$1"; shift
  local logfile="session_logs/wave2-gpu${gpu}-${name}.log"
  echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] Launching GPU $gpu: $name → $logfile"
  CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="$gpu" SENPAI_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=720 \
    nohup python train.py \
      --epochs "$EPOCHS" \
      --agent "$AGENT" \
      --wandb_group "$GROUP" \
      --wandb_name "${GROUP}/${name}" \
      "$@" > "$logfile" 2>&1 &
  echo $! > "session_logs/wave2-gpu${gpu}-${name}.pid"
}

# GPU 0: subsample baseline (controls for train/eval distribution shift).
start_run 0 sub32k-default \
  --subsample 32000 --surf_oversample 0.5

# GPU 1: subsample baseline + Fourier (small-model fourier control with sub).
start_run 1 sub32k-default-fourier \
  --subsample 32000 --surf_oversample 0.5 \
  --fourier_freq 16 --fourier_scale 5.0

# GPU 2: subsample h256/l8/mlp4 (mid model, no extras).
start_run 2 sub32k-h256-l8-mlp4 \
  --subsample 32000 --surf_oversample 0.5 \
  --n_hidden 256 --n_layers 8 --n_head 8 --mlp_ratio 4

# GPU 3: subsample h256/l8/mlp4 + Fourier.
start_run 3 sub32k-h256-l8-mlp4-fourier \
  --subsample 32000 --surf_oversample 0.5 \
  --n_hidden 256 --n_layers 8 --n_head 8 --mlp_ratio 4 \
  --fourier_freq 16 --fourier_scale 5.0

# GPU 4: subsample h256/l8/mlp4 + Fourier + Transolver++ (Ada-Temp + Rep-Slice).
start_run 4 sub32k-h256-l8-mlp4-fourier-tpp \
  --subsample 32000 --surf_oversample 0.5 \
  --n_hidden 256 --n_layers 8 --n_head 8 --mlp_ratio 4 \
  --fourier_freq 16 --fourier_scale 5.0 \
  --use_ada_temp True --use_rep_slice True

# GPU 5: subsample h256/l8/mlp4 + Fourier + Ada-Temp ONLY (no Rep-Slice).
# Test whether the noise from Rep-Slice is helpful or just adds variance.
start_run 5 sub32k-h256-l8-mlp4-fourier-ada \
  --subsample 32000 --surf_oversample 0.5 \
  --n_hidden 256 --n_layers 8 --n_head 8 --mlp_ratio 4 \
  --fourier_freq 16 --fourier_scale 5.0 \
  --use_ada_temp True

# GPU 6: deeper model — n_hidden=256, n_layers=12, mlp_ratio=4.
start_run 6 sub32k-h256-l12-mlp4-fourier-ada \
  --subsample 32000 --surf_oversample 0.5 \
  --n_hidden 256 --n_layers 12 --n_head 8 --mlp_ratio 4 \
  --fourier_freq 16 --fourier_scale 5.0 \
  --use_ada_temp True

# GPU 7: wider model — n_hidden=384, n_layers=8, mlp_ratio=4.
start_run 7 sub32k-h384-l8-mlp4-fourier-ada \
  --subsample 32000 --surf_oversample 0.5 \
  --n_hidden 384 --n_layers 8 --n_head 8 --mlp_ratio 4 \
  --fourier_freq 16 --fourier_scale 5.0 \
  --use_ada_temp True

echo "Wave 2 launched."
"}

function.arguments:
{"command": "cd /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target && python -c "
import torch
import json
from pathlib import Path
import collections

# Look at per-domain stats
splits = '/mnt/new-pvc/datasets/tandemfoil/splits_v2'
with open(f'{splits}/meta.json') as f: meta = json.load(f)
print('Domain groups:', list(meta['domain_groups'].keys()))
print('Sizes:', {k: len(v) for k,v in meta['domain_groups'].items()})
print()

# Look at one sample from each domain
domains = ['racecar_single', 'racecar_tandem', 'cruise_tandem']
import os
files = sorted(os.listdir(f'{splits}/train'))
for d, idxs in meta['domain_groups'].items():
    print(f'\\nDomain: {d}, {len(idxs)} samples')
    sample = torch.load(f'{splits}/train/{files[idxs[0]]}', weights_only=True)
    x, y, surf = sample['x'], sample['y'], sample['is_surface']
    print(f'  N nodes: {x.shape[0]}, surface: {surf.sum().item()} ({surf.float().mean().item()*100:.2f}%)')
    print(f'  x[:,0] range: [{x[:,0].min():.2f}, {x[:,0].max():.2f}] (x coord)')
    print(f'  x[:,1] range: [{x[:,1].min():.2f}, {x[:,1].max():.2f}] (z coord)')
    # surface only
    sx = x[surf]
    sy = y[surf]
    print(f'  surf x[:,0] range: [{sx[:,0].min():.2f}, {sx[:,0].max():.2f}]')
    print(f'  surf x[:,1] range: [{sx[:,1].min():.2f}, {sx[:,1].max():.2f}]')
    print(f'  y[:,2] (p) on surface: mean={sy[:,2].mean():.2f}, std={sy[:,2].std():.2f}, min={sy[:,2].min():.2f}, max={sy[:,2].max():.2f}')
    print(f'  Re (x[:,13]): {x[0,13].item():.2f} (log Re = {x[0,13].item()*1.43+14.58:.2f})')
"", "description": "Inspect per-domain data"}

1. Are there known tricks beyond what I already have (Fourier features, surface-weighted loss, Ada-Temp/Rep-Slice, scaling) that specifically improve surface pressure?

2. Is there evidence for or against:
  - L1 vs L2 loss for pressure regression (heavy tails)
  - Multi-scale predictors (e.g. predict residual from mean prediction)
  - Auxiliary losses (e.g. predict mean pressure first, then refine)
  - EMA model weights for evaluation
  - Different optimizers (Lion vs AdamW, longer warmup)

3. For a small dataset (~1500 train samples) with surface MAE as the metric: what's the recommended training-time augmentation? Random rotations, scaling, reflections — which preserve physics for airfoil surrogate models?

4. Do any recent papers report Transolver (or similar) checkpoint averaging or ensembling improvements?

messages.content:
I have sufficient data from the literature. Let me compile the comprehensive findings.

---

# Research Report: Improving Surface Pressure MAE on TandemFoilSet with Transolver

## SOTA Landscape (2024–2026)

**Architecture ranking on surface pressure (rel. L2):**
- Transolver-3 (2602.04940, Feb 2026): NASA-CRM p_s = **8.71%** rel-L2 — SOTA on Transolver family
- AB-UPT (2502.09692): NASA-CRM p_s = 9.77%, DrivAerML p_s = 3.82% — strong on large industrial datasets
- Transolver++ → Transolver-3: 9.51% → 8.71% gain from restoring the Linear1 projection in slice domain
- MARIO (2505.14704): **~10× lower MSE than Transolver/GNNs** on AirfRANS scarce (200 samples) — but different architecture
- On 3D transonic wings (2511.21474): Transolver/AB-UPT/ViT all score **0.002 rel-L2 for surface p** in-distribution — nearly indistinguishable at model scale

---

## Question-by-Question Findings

---

### 1. Beyond Your Current Tricks: What Actually Moves the Needle

**A. Boundary Layer Mask as Explicit Input Feature** *(MARIO, 2505.14704)*
The biggest single win reported for surface pressure on the scarce AirfRANS task is the combination of:
- SDF (signed distance function) as input node feature
- A **boundary layer mask** σ_bl derived from SDF: `σ_bl = threshold(normalize(1/|sdf|), τ=0.02)` — a soft binary that highlights nodes within ~2% chord of the wall
- This is fed as an *additional input channel*, not just as a loss weight

Result: MARIO with this feature achieves p_s surface MSE of 0.27×10⁻¹ vs Transolver 2.367×10⁻¹ (8.8× better) on 200-sample scarce task. If you aren't using SDF + boundary-layer mask as input features, **this is your largest uncaptured gain**.

**B. Riemannian Geometric Features for Cp** *(2401.09452)*
For surface Cp specifically on 3D wings: Riemannian metric/connection/curvature (from Bézier surface fit) as node features fed into a Deep Attention Network improved MSE significantly over coordinate-only baselines. Practically: curvature of the airfoil surface as an additional input. For Transolver, this means precomputing and appending κ (principal curvatures) to the node feature vector.

**C. Area-Weighted Sampling + Area-Weighted Auxiliary Loss** *(DoMINO, 2501.13350)*
DoMINO uses **two explicit mechanisms** for surface fidelity:
1. `area_weighted_sampling`: surface nodes are sampled proportionally to their facet area (not uniform), ensuring small-area high-gradient regions near leading/trailing edges get representation
2. An **area-weighted MSE term** added to the total loss: `L_total = MSE_volume + MSE_surface + MSE_area_weighted_surface`. The area-weighted term acts as an additional signal for drag/lift accuracy.

This is orthogonal to your current surface-weighted loss (λ × (1/|S|) Σ‖·‖²): you want **area-weighted sampling** at the node level AND **area-weighted loss** as a separate term.

**D. Conditioning Injection via FiLM/AdaLN into Every Block** *(SMART, 2601.18707; Aero-Nef, 2407.19916)*
Modulated positional encoding (MPE) where γ(ξ), β(ξ) = MLP(AoA, Re, Mach) scales/shifts the sinusoidal positional features per-sample. Evidence from SMART's ablation (Appendix K.2): modulated PE consistently outperforms standard sinusoidal PE. For Transolver: inject AoA/Re as AdaLN scale+shift in every Physics-Attention block, not just at input. This is distinct from simply appending scalars to node features.

**E. Restoring Linear1 in Physics-Attention** *(Transolver-3, 2602.04940)*
Transolver++ *removed* Linear1 to save memory. Transolver-3 *restores* it (moved to slice domain). This alone drives the 9.51% → 8.71% improvement on NASA-CRM. If you're using Transolver++ internals, **add Linear1 back** (Eq. 3 in 2602.04940). O(MC²) not O(NC²) after the fix.

---

### 2. Specific Technique Evidence

**L1 vs L2 Loss:**
No paper in this corpus directly ablates L1 vs L2 for surface pressure. All 2024–2026 papers use **MSE (L2)** exclusively:
- Transolver-3: relative L2 loss
- AB-UPT: MSE
- DoMINO: MSE per field
- Aero-Nef (2511.21474): MSE for all fields

**Recommendation**: No published evidence for L1 outperforming L2 on RANS pressure. However, the pressure field at leading-edge stagnation and shock locations *does* have heavy tails. **Huber loss (δ=0.1–1.0 in normalized units)** is the theoretically motivated choice but is untested in this domain. If you observe large outliers on sharp pressure peaks (leading edge, suction peak), try Huber. Keep the surface-weighted term: `L = MSE_vol + λ·Huber_surf` with λ~10–50 for the 2% node fraction.

**Multi-Scale / Residual Predictors:**
No paper in 2024–2026 directly implements "predict mean then refine" for airfoil pressure. However, MARIO's **resolution-invariant training** (train on 5000 subsampled nodes, infer on 200K full mesh) implicitly learns a smooth global field first. The closest architectural analog in this corpus is **Transolver-3's physical state caching**: the M slices capture a coarse mean-field physical state; decoding reconstructs fine-scale variation. 

*Actionable*: No published evidence for auxiliary mean-prediction head improving Transolver surface MAE. The slice-based architecture already provides this separation. However, adding a **global coefficient head** (predict C_L, C_D from the physical states before decoding) as an auxiliary loss has soft support from Transolver-3 showing R² > 0.99 for C_d when trained end-to-end.

**Auxiliary Losses:**
- DoMINO: area-weighted surface loss as auxiliary = confirmed beneficial for drag/lift accuracy
- AirfRANS (2212.07564) explicit formula: `L = (1/|V|)Σ‖f(xᵢ)-yᵢ‖² + λ(1/|S|)Σ‖f(xᵢ)-yᵢ‖²` with λ=1 (in their disclaimer: due to a bug they actually trained without this; **their λ>1 results are in Appendix N** — they found surface-weighted loss in Appendix N gives lower surface errors but did not systematically ablate λ values)
- **Conclusion**: Surface-weighted loss is beneficial (published), λ should be >> 1 given your 2% node fraction. Recommendation: λ = |V|/|S| ≈ 50 to equalize gradients per node.

**EMA model weights:**
Zero published evidence in this domain. EMA is standard in diffusion models (score matching) but not reported in any 2024–2026 CFD surrogate paper. **However**: the 3-seed standard deviations in Table 2 of (2511.21474) are tiny (±0.0000–0.0005), suggesting the training signal is stable. EMA with decay 0.999 is low-risk and worth 1 run — standard PyTorch `torch.optim.swa_utils.AveragedModel` or timm's ModelEMA. No published evidence of improvement, but also no evidence against.

**Optimizer: Lion vs AdamW:**
Directly relevant finding from Transolver-3 (2602.04940, Appendix A.4):
> "we employed the AdamW optimizer... except for AB-UPT/UPT where LION was used. For AdamW... lr sweep over {1e-3, 5e-4, 2e-4, 1e-4, 5e-5, 2e-5}. Whenever LION was applied, lr = 5e-5"

Both work comparably when tuned. AB-UPT (Lion, lr=5e-5) and Transolver (AdamW, lr=1e-3→decay) perform within 1% on NASA-CRM. On transonic wings (2511.21474): **Lion optimizer with lr=1e-5, weight_decay=0.05, grad_clip=0.25, cosine decay with 5% linear warmup**.

**Longer warmup**: The 2511.21474 paper uses 5% of training steps as warmup (= 500 steps for 10k steps/epoch training). Transolver-3 uses 5% warmup + cosine to 1e-6. For 1500 samples × ~500 epochs with batch=1, that's ~750K steps total — 5% warmup = ~37K steps ≈ ~50 epochs. Your current warmup should cover ~5% of total steps.

**Specialized initialization/normalization:**
- Z-score normalization of all fields is universal (2511.21474, Transolver-3)
- Exception: vorticity is normalized by average magnitude (2511.21474) — relevant if you also predict skin friction
- **bfloat16 vs float16**: Transolver-3 found "depending on which yielded better results" — for pressure on small datasets, float32 or bfloat16 is safer; GINO showed instability under mixed precision
- No specialized initialization schemes reported beyond standard Xavier/Kaiming in any paper

---

### 3. Training-Time Augmentation for Airfoil Surrogates (~1500 samples)

**Directly physics-preserving augmentations (safe):**

| Augmentation | Physics-preserving? | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| **y-axis reflection (α → -α, u_y → -u_y, p unchanged)** | ✅ Exact | AirfRANS spans α∈[-5°, 15°]; negative AoA mirrored = positive AoA with flipped camber. If your dataset has symmetric airfoils, this doubles effective data exactly |
| **Chord-direction scaling (x→cx)** | ⚠️ Only if Re scaled too | Changes aspect ratio → changes pressure distribution unless Re, Mach adjusted |
| **Random rotation** | ❌ Breaks physics | Freestream direction changes; p, u fields do NOT transform trivially |
| **x-translation** | ✅ Only if periodic BC | For non-periodic domains, safe only if the translated geometry stays within the cropped domain |
| **Gaussian noise on geometry coordinates** | ⚠️ Small only | σ < 0.001c (chord) — adds regularization, but large noise creates non-physical geometries |

**Best augmentation for your case**: If TandemFoilSet-Balanced has symmetric airfoils (tandem = two lifting surfaces, likely non-symmetric at non-zero AoA):
1. **y-reflection augmentation**: For each sample (geometry, AoA=α, p, u), add synthetic sample (geometry_reflected, AoA=-α, p_sym, u_y_negated). Exact physical invariance. This doubles your effective dataset: 1500 → 3000.
2. **Random subsetting of mesh nodes per epoch** (stochastic point dropout): Used by Aero-Nef, MARIO, Transolver-3. At each epoch sample ~70–85% of nodes randomly. Acts as regularization preventing overfit to specific mesh topology.
3. **Multi-resolution training**: MARIO trains on 5000/200K nodes, Transolver-3 uses geometry amortized subsets. For 1500 samples this means sampling random subsets of surface nodes per batch.

**Do NOT use**: random rotation/scaling of full geometry without adjusting the freestream condition parameters — this changes the physics.

---

### 4. Checkpoint Averaging / Ensembling

**No published evidence** in 2024–2026 for SWA or checkpoint ensembling specifically for Transolver or similar CFD surrogates. The multi-seed standard deviations from (2511.21474) are extremely low (σ≈0.0000–0.0005 for p_s), suggesting:
- Individual runs are stable → ensembling gives <0.05% gain
- The signal is dominated by architecture and data, not optimization noise

**However**: Multi-seed ensembling (3 seeds → average predictions) is the de-facto evaluation protocol used in all 2024–2026 papers. Running 3 seeds and averaging at inference is low-cost on 8 GPUs and provides free ~0.1–0.3% improvement per the variance seen in tables.

---

## Ranked Recipe Table

| Rank | Technique | Evidence | Expected Impact | Cost |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **1** | **BL mask input + SDF feature** | MARIO (2505.14704): 8.8× surface p improvement on scarce task | **High** | 1 feature col + 1 run |
| **2** | **Area-weighted sampling + area-weighted auxiliary loss** | DoMINO (2501.13350): area-weighted rel-L2 < rel-L2 for all surface fields | **High** | Loss + sampler change |
| **3** | **λ = |V|/|S| ≈ 50 for surface loss** | AirfRANS (2212.07564) Appendix N: surface-weighted loss reduces surface errors | **Medium-High** | 1 hyperparam |
| **4** | **Restore Linear1 in Physics-Attention** | Transolver-3 (2602.04940): 9.51→8.71% rel-L2 | **Medium** | Architecture change |
| **5** | **y-reflection augmentation** | Physics invariance, doubles dataset | **Medium** (1500→3000 effective) | Preprocessing |
| **6** | **Per-block AoA/Re FiLM conditioning** | SMART ablation K.2 (2601.18707): MPE > standard PE | **Medium** | Architecture |
| **7** | **Curvature features as node input** | 2401.09452: significant CP improvement on 3D wings | **Low-Medium** | Preprocessing |
| **8** | **Lion optimizer, lr=1e-5, wd=0.05, clip=0.25** | 2511.21474: works for Transolver/AB-UPT on wings | **Low** (vs tuned AdamW) | 1 hyperparam |
| **9** | **3-seed ensemble** | 2511.21474: σ≈0.0000 → free ~0.1% | **Very Low** | 3× compute |

---

## Priority Recommendation

**Run 1 (highest leverage, within budget)**: Add SDF + boundary-layer mask `σ_bl = clip(normalize(1/sdf), 0, 1) * (sdf < τ)` (τ=0.02) as input channels to each node. Simultaneously bump λ from current value to `|total_nodes|/|surface_nodes| ≈ 50`. Expected: 30–50%+ surface MAE reduction based on MARIO's results on identical problem class (scarce 2D airfoil, ~200 training samples).

**Run 2**: Add area-weighted sampling: at each epoch, oversample surface nodes with probability ∝ face area (precompute face areas from mesh). Add a second loss term: `L_aw = MSE_surface_area_weighted`. This specifically pushes accuracy at leading/trailing edges where face area is smallest (and pressure gradients largest).

**Run 3**: y-reflection augmentation (if airfoils in TandemFoilSet are approximately symmetric). For each (geom, AoA, p, u_x, u_y) → add (geom_flip_y, -AoA, p_sym, u_x, -u_y).

**Do not prioritize**: Lion vs AdamW swap, multi-scale auxiliary heads, or EMA — no published evidence of meaningful gains on this problem class, and the 24h budget is better spent on the high-evidence items.

---

## Key Code Patterns

```python
# Boundary layer mask (from MARIO/2505.14704)
# sdf: [N,] signed distance from surface (positive = outside airfoil)
tau = 0.02  # 2% chord
sdf_norm = 1.0 / (sdf.abs() + 1e-6)  # inverse distance
sdf_norm = sdf_norm / sdf_norm.max()   # normalize to [0,1]
bl_mask = (sdf.abs() < tau).float() * sdf_norm  # soft BL indicator [N,]
node_features = torch.cat([coords, normals, sdf, bl_mask.unsqueeze(-1), vel_bc], dim=-1)

# Area-weighted surface loss (from DoMINO/2501.13350)
# face_areas: [N_surf,] area of each surface facet
def surface_loss(pred_surf, gt_surf, face_areas):
    mse = (pred_surf - gt_surf).pow(2).mean(dim=-1)  # [N_surf,]
    weighted = (face_areas / face_areas.sum()) * mse
    return mse.mean() + weighted.sum()  # uniform + area-weighted

# Surface-weighted combined loss (from AirfRANS/2212.07564)
# lambda_s = |V| / |S|  ≈ 50 for 2% surface fraction
loss = mse_volume + lambda_s * mse_surface

# y-axis reflection augmentation for negative AoA
def reflect_augment(coords, velocity, pressure, aoa):
    # flip y-coordinate of all nodes
    coords_aug = coords.clone(); coords_aug[:, 1] *= -1
    # negate y-velocity, pressure unchanged (scalar)
    vel_aug = velocity.clone(); vel_aug[:, 1] *= -1
    aoa_aug = -aoa
    return coords_aug, vel_aug, pressure, aoa_aug

# Transolver-3 training config (from 2602.04940, Appendix A.4)
optimizer = AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=1e-3, weight_decay=0.05)
scheduler = CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=500, eta_min=1e-6)
# 5% warmup: use LinearLR for first 25 epochs, then cosine

# Lion optimizer config (from 2511.21474 Appendix B)
from lion_pytorch import Lion
optimizer = Lion(model.parameters(), lr=1e-5, weight_decay=0.05)
# gradient clipping: torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 0.25)
# precision: float16, cosine decay, 5% linear warmup
```

---

## Essential References

| Paper | arXiv | Key Finding |
|---|---|---|
| MARIO | 2505.14704 | BL mask + SDF → 8.8× surface p gain (scarce 2D airfoil) |
| DoMINO | 2501.13350 | Area-weighted loss + sampling for surface fields |
| AirfRANS | 2212.07564 | λ-weighted surface loss formula (Eq. 3); Appendix N has results |
| Transolver-3 | 2602.04940 | Linear1 restoration; config table A.4; λ sweep |
| Transonic Wings (AB-UPT) | 2511.21474 | Appendix B: Lion lr=1e-5, clip=0.25, 5% warmup, bfloat16 |
| SMART | 2601.18707 | Appendix K.2: MPE ablation; FiLM conditioning |
| Riemannian Cp | 2401.09452 | Curvature features for Cp on 3D wings |

    # Train-time augmentation. Stochastic y-axis reflection that negates the

function.arguments:
{"path": "/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/train.py", "old_str": "def subsample_batch(
    x: torch.Tensor,
    y: torch.Tensor,
    is_surface: torch.Tensor,
    mask: torch.Tensor,
    n_sub: int,
    surf_oversample: float = 0.5,
):", "new_str": "def yflip_batch(
    x: torch.Tensor,
    y: torch.Tensor,
    mask: torch.Tensor,
    p_flip: float = 0.5,
) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
    """Stochastic y-axis (z-coordinate) reflection per sample in a batch.

    Physics: reflecting z → -z is a valid symmetry of the airfoil flow if we
    also negate (a) the foil AoAs (radians), (b) the y-velocity target Uy,
    and (c) any geometric quantities tied to chord-direction sign. NACA shape
    parameters (camber/position/thickness) are scalar magnitudes — invariant.
    Pressure is a scalar — invariant. Re is invariant. Gap is a magnitude —
    invariant. Stagger sign depends on convention; we negate it conservatively.

    Affects only valid (mask=True) nodes; padding stays untouched.

    Modifies in place; returns the same tensors for chained ops.
    """
    if p_flip <= 0:
        return x, y
    B = x.shape[0]
    flip = torch.rand(B, device=x.device) < p_flip
    if not flip.any():
        return x, y
    f_idx = flip.nonzero(as_tuple=False).flatten()
    # Avoid mask handling complications: only flip valid mesh entries.
    # Fields to negate (indices in x):
    #   1: z coordinate
    #   14: foil 1 AoA (radians)
    #   18: foil 2 AoA (radians)
    #   23: stagger (sign-dependent geometric offset)
    # Fields in y:
    #   1: Uy (y-velocity)
    for b in f_idx.tolist():
        m = mask[b]
        x[b, m, 1] = -x[b, m, 1]
        x[b, m, 14] = -x[b, m, 14]
        x[b, m, 18] = -x[b, m, 18]
        x[b, m, 23] = -x[b, m, 23]
        y[b, m, 1] = -y[b, m, 1]
    return x, y


def subsample_batch(
    x: torch.Tensor,
    y: torch.Tensor,
    is_surface: torch.Tensor,
    mask: torch.Tensor,
    n_sub: int,
    surf_oversample: float = 0.5,
):"}

function.arguments:
{"path": "/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/scripts/wave3.sh", "content": "#!/bin/bash
# Wave 3: build on the warmup-clip win.
#
# Top 2 configs from waves 1+2 both used warmup_frac=0.05, grad_clip=1.0
# (val_avg/mae_surf_p ~96 at ep18 vs vanilla 116). Build on that.
#
# Goals:
#   1. Confirm warmup-clip recipe is robust → multi-seed.
#   2. Compose with Fourier features (which led briefly mid-training).
#   3. Combine with subsampling+bigger model to test capacity.
#   4. Try surf_weight=50 (paper-recommended ~|V|/|S| ≈ 50).
#   5. Push longer training to 100 epochs on the best small config.
set -e
cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."
mkdir -p session_logs

GROUP="mlintern-pai2-24h-v2-r1"
AGENT="ml-intern-r1"

start_run() {
  local gpu="$1"; shift
  local name="$1"; shift
  local logfile="session_logs/wave3-gpu${gpu}-${name}.log"
  echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] Launching GPU $gpu: $name → $logfile"
  CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="$gpu" SENPAI_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=720 \
    nohup python train.py \
      --agent "$AGENT" \
      --wandb_group "$GROUP" \
      --wandb_name "${GROUP}/${name}" \
      "$@" > "$logfile" 2>&1 &
  echo $! > "session_logs/wave3-gpu${gpu}-${name}.pid"
}

# GPU 0: best-small + 100 epochs (confirm that more epochs helps).
start_run 0 wc-100ep-seed0 \
  --epochs 100 \
  --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0 --seed 0

# GPU 1: best-small + 100 epochs + Fourier features (combine).
start_run 1 wc-fourier-100ep-seed0 \
  --epochs 100 \
  --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0 --seed 0 \
  --fourier_freq 16 --fourier_scale 5.0

# GPU 2: best-small + surf_weight=50 + 100 epochs (per AirfRANS / DoMINO).
start_run 2 wc-sw50-100ep-seed0 \
  --epochs 100 \
  --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0 --seed 0 \
  --surf_weight 50

# GPU 3: best-small + Fourier + surf_weight=50 + 100 epochs.
start_run 3 wc-sw50-fourier-100ep-seed0 \
  --epochs 100 \
  --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0 --seed 0 \
  --fourier_freq 16 --fourier_scale 5.0 \
  --surf_weight 50

# GPU 4: bigger model (h256/l8/mlp4) + sub32k + warmup-clip recipe + 100 epochs.
start_run 4 wc-sub32k-h256-l8-mlp4-100ep \
  --epochs 100 \
  --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0 \
  --subsample 32000 --surf_oversample 0.3 \
  --n_hidden 256 --n_layers 8 --n_head 8 --mlp_ratio 4

# GPU 5: bigger model + Fourier + warmup-clip recipe + 100 epochs.
start_run 5 wc-sub32k-h256-l8-mlp4-fourier-100ep \
  --epochs 100 \
  --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0 \
  --subsample 32000 --surf_oversample 0.3 \
  --n_hidden 256 --n_layers 8 --n_head 8 --mlp_ratio 4 \
  --fourier_freq 16 --fourier_scale 5.0

# GPU 6: bigger model + Fourier + surf_weight=50 + 100 epochs.
start_run 6 wc-sub32k-h256-l8-mlp4-fourier-sw50-100ep \
  --epochs 100 \
  --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0 \
  --subsample 32000 --surf_oversample 0.3 \
  --n_hidden 256 --n_layers 8 --n_head 8 --mlp_ratio 4 \
  --fourier_freq 16 --fourier_scale 5.0 \
  --surf_weight 50

# GPU 7: best-small + multi-seed (variance check).
start_run 7 wc-100ep-seed1 \
  --epochs 100 \
  --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0 --seed 1

echo "Wave 3 launched."
"}

messages.content: Let me add Lion optimizer (inline implementation, no new package dependency) for testing in Wave 3:

function.arguments:
{"path": "/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/train.py", "old_str": "def _set_seed(seed: int) -> None:
    random.seed(seed)
    np.random.seed(seed)
    torch.manual_seed(seed)
    torch.cuda.manual_seed_all(seed)", "new_str": "def _set_seed(seed: int) -> None:
    random.seed(seed)
    np.random.seed(seed)
    torch.manual_seed(seed)
    torch.cuda.manual_seed_all(seed)


class Lion(torch.optim.Optimizer):
    """EvoLved Sign Momentum (Chen et al. 2023, arXiv 2302.06675).

    Decoupled-WD sign-momentum optimizer; reported to give comparable or
    better results than AdamW at lower learning rates, with smaller memory
    footprint (one moment buffer instead of two). Used by AB-UPT
    (arXiv 2502.09692) and the 3D transonic wing benchmark
    (arXiv 2511.21474) for Transolver-class CFD surrogates.
    """

    def __init__(self, params, lr: float = 1e-4, betas=(0.9, 0.99), weight_decay: float = 0.0):
        if lr < 0:
            raise ValueError(f"Invalid learning rate: {lr}")
        if not 0.0 <= betas[0] < 1.0 or not 0.0 <= betas[1] < 1.0:
            raise ValueError(f"Invalid betas: {betas}")
        defaults = dict(lr=lr, betas=betas, weight_decay=weight_decay)
        super().__init__(params, defaults)

    @torch.no_grad()
    def step(self, closure=None):
        loss = None
        if closure is not None:
            with torch.enable_grad():
                loss = closure()
        for group in self.param_groups:
            lr = group["lr"]
            wd = group["weight_decay"]
            beta1, beta2 = group["betas"]
            for p in group["params"]:
                if p.grad is None:
                    continue
                # Decoupled weight decay
                if wd != 0:
                    p.data.mul_(1.0 - lr * wd)
                grad = p.grad
                state = self.state[p]
                if len(state) == 0:
                    state["exp_avg"] = torch.zeros_like(p)
                exp_avg = state["exp_avg"]
                # update direction = sign(beta1 * exp_avg + (1 - beta1) * grad)
                update = exp_avg.mul(beta1).add(grad, alpha=1 - beta1).sign_()
                p.data.add_(update, alpha=-lr)
                # Update the running momentum with beta2 (decoupled from update).
                exp_avg.mul_(beta2).add_(grad, alpha=1 - beta2)
        return loss"}

{"command": "cd /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target && python -c "
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '.')
from train import Lion

m = nn.Linear(10, 5)
opt = Lion(m.parameters(), lr=1e-4, weight_decay=0.05)
x = torch.randn(8, 10)
y = torch.randn(8, 5)
for _ in range(3):
    pred = m(x)
    loss = ((pred - y)**2).mean()
    opt.zero_grad()
    loss.backward()
    opt.step()
print('Lion CPU test ok, weights moved')
print('Sample weight:', m.weight.flatten()[0].item())
"", "description": "Lion CPU test"}

messages.content: GPU 6 with lr=1e-3 + warmup-clip is now leading at 85.07! Let me update wave 3 to incorporate this:

function.arguments:
{"path": "/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/scripts/wave3.sh", "content": "#!/bin/bash
# Wave 3: build on the warmup-clip + lr=1e-3 win.
#
# Best run so far: default-lr1e3-warmup-clip ep22 = 85.07
# Best small config: --lr 1e-3 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0
#
# Goals:
#   1. Confirm warmup-clip + lr=1e-3 is robust → multi-seed.
#   2. Compose with Fourier features.
#   3. Combine with subsampling+bigger model to test capacity ceiling.
#   4. Try surf_weight=50 (paper-recommended ~|V|/|S| ≈ 50).
#   5. Push to 100 epochs on the best small config.
set -e
cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."
mkdir -p session_logs

GROUP="mlintern-pai2-24h-v2-r1"
AGENT="ml-intern-r1"

start_run() {
  local gpu="$1"; shift
  local name="$1"; shift
  local logfile="session_logs/wave3-gpu${gpu}-${name}.log"
  echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] Launching GPU $gpu: $name → $logfile"
  CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="$gpu" SENPAI_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=720 \
    nohup python train.py \
      --agent "$AGENT" \
      --wandb_group "$GROUP" \
      --wandb_name "${GROUP}/${name}" \
      "$@" > "$logfile" 2>&1 &
  echo $! > "session_logs/wave3-gpu${gpu}-${name}.pid"
}

# Common best-small recipe: --lr 1e-3 --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0
WC="--warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0"
LR="--lr 1e-3"

# GPU 0: best-small + 100 epochs (extend past 50, see if cosine continues to gain).
start_run 0 wc-lr1e3-100ep-seed0 \
  --epochs 100 $WC $LR --seed 0

# GPU 1: best-small + 100 epochs + Fourier features.
start_run 1 wc-lr1e3-fourier-100ep-seed0 \
  --epochs 100 $WC $LR --seed 0 \
  --fourier_freq 16 --fourier_scale 5.0

# GPU 2: best-small + surf_weight=50 (per AirfRANS / DoMINO).
start_run 2 wc-lr1e3-sw50-100ep-seed0 \
  --epochs 100 $WC $LR --seed 0 \
  --surf_weight 50

# GPU 3: best-small + Fourier + surf_weight=50.
start_run 3 wc-lr1e3-sw50-fourier-100ep-seed0 \
  --epochs 100 $WC $LR --seed 0 \
  --fourier_freq 16 --fourier_scale 5.0 \
  --surf_weight 50

# GPU 4: bigger model (h256/l8/mlp4) + sub32k + warmup-clip recipe.
# surf_oversample 0.3 (closer to eval distribution's 2% than 0.5).
start_run 4 wc-lr1e3-sub32k-h256-l8-mlp4-100ep \
  --epochs 100 $WC $LR \
  --subsample 32000 --surf_oversample 0.3 \
  --n_hidden 256 --n_layers 8 --n_head 8 --mlp_ratio 4

# GPU 5: bigger model + Fourier.
start_run 5 wc-lr1e3-sub32k-h256-l8-mlp4-fourier-100ep \
  --epochs 100 $WC $LR \
  --subsample 32000 --surf_oversample 0.3 \
  --n_hidden 256 --n_layers 8 --n_head 8 --mlp_ratio 4 \
  --fourier_freq 16 --fourier_scale 5.0

# GPU 6: bigger model + Fourier + surf_weight=50.
start_run 6 wc-lr1e3-sub32k-h256-l8-mlp4-fourier-sw50-100ep \
  --epochs 100 $WC $LR \
  --subsample 32000 --surf_oversample 0.3 \
  --n_hidden 256 --n_layers 8 --n_head 8 --mlp_ratio 4 \
  --fourier_freq 16 --fourier_scale 5.0 \
  --surf_weight 50

# GPU 7: best-small + multi-seed (variance check).
start_run 7 wc-lr1e3-100ep-seed1 \
  --epochs 100 $WC $LR --seed 1

echo "Wave 3 launched."
"}

messages.content: Let me start drafting the summary while waiting:

function.arguments:
{"path": "/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/research/MLINTERN_SUMMARY.md", "content": "# ML Intern — TandemFoilSet-Balanced Benchmark Summary

**Branch:** `mlintern-pai2-24h-v2-r1` · **W&B project:** `wandb-applied-ai-team/senpai-v1-ml-intern`
**Date:** 2026-04-29 → 2026-04-30 · **GPU budget:** 8 × NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell (96 GB) · **Wall budget:** 24h

> _This document is updated incrementally during the run. Final version pushed at the end._

## TL;DR

The leading recipe is the baseline Transolver (n_hidden=128, n_layers=5, n_head=4, slice_num=64,
mlp_ratio=2) trained with **5 % linear warmup + cosine decay + grad-clip 1.0 at lr=1e-3**.
That single 50-epoch run reached `val_avg/mae_surf_p ≈ 73` mid-run vs ~140 for the
unmodified baseline at the same epoch. Final test metric is filled in below once the run
completes its end-of-run test pass.

## Strategy

1. **Read the program/SPLITS docs** first; treat `data/` as read-only and only edit `train.py`.
2. **Pull the recipe from the literature** (`Transolver` arXiv 2402.02366,
   `Transolver++` 2502.02414, `MARIO` 2505.14704, `AB-UPT` 2502.09692,
   `DoMINO` 2501.13350, `Transolver-3` 2602.04940) — see
   `research/MLINTERN_RESULTS.jsonl` and the in-line comments in `train.py`.
3. **Expose the relevant hyperparameters** in `train.py` (model size, optimizer,
   schedule, surface weights, Fourier features, Transolver++ ada-temp / rep-slice,
   subsampling, AMP, optional Lion).
4. **Wave-based ablation** on 8 parallel GPUs:
   - **Wave 1**: scan major axes — capacity, Transolver++ flags, pressure weight,
     Fourier PE, warmup+clip, lr=1e-3.
   - **Wave 2**: bigger model + train-time mesh subsampling (32 K nodes/sample)
     to fit larger Transolvers in 96 GB while still using the full mesh at eval.
   - **Wave 3**: confirm the warmup+clip+lr=1e-3 win and combine it with the best
     extras (Fourier, surf_weight=50, subsampling+bigger model). 100-epoch budget.
5. **Hardware pinning**: every job pinned to a specific GPU via `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES`.

## Key changes to `train.py`

These are framework changes; the *default* CLI invocation (no flags) reproduces the
original baseline up to one harmless correctness fix (mask zero-out of padded nodes
in the slice attention).

- **Model knobs as CLI flags**: `n_hidden`, `n_layers`, `n_head`, `slice_num`,
  `mlp_ratio`, `dropout`.
- **Transolver++ ablations** (`use_ada_temp`, `use_rep_slice`) with NaN-safe
  clamps on the per-point temperature and the Gumbel noise tail.
- **Fourier positional encoding** of normalized (x, z) coordinates, gated by
  `fourier_freq` (and `fourier_scale`).
- **Per-channel surface weight** (`surf_p_weight`) plus the global `surf_weight`.
- **Optimizer choice** (`adamw` / `adam` / `lion`) and **LR schedule** (`cosine`
  with optional `warmup_frac`) and **grad clip** (`grad_clip`).
- **AMP** (`bf16` / `fp16`).
- **Train-time mesh subsampling** (`subsample`, `surf_oversample`) with
  surface-biased sampling so big models fit while eval still runs on full meshes.
- **y-axis flip augmentation** function (`yflip_batch`); kept off by default
  because the dataset's z is positive-only and the dsdf features are not
  guaranteed to be sign-invariant — so a free reflection is not a safe symmetry.
- **Mask-aware physics attention** (zeros out padded nodes when forming slice
  tokens) to match the masked downstream loss.
- **Plain stdout epoch lines** with a `disable=True` tqdm so logs are greppable.
- Saved hyperparameters into the W&B model artifact metadata.

`data/`, `data/scoring.py`, the scoring contract, normalization, and the
`{x_norm, mask}` model interface are unchanged.

## Best runs (live, will be finalized)

See `research/MLINTERN_RESULTS.jsonl` for one JSON object per run. Use
`python scripts/collect_results.py` to refresh it from `session_logs/*.log`.

| Rank | Run                        | val_avg/mae_surf_p | Test avg | Status   |
|----:|----------------------------|-------------------:|---------:|----------|
| 1   | default-lr1e3-warmup-clip  | (in progress)      | (pending)| running  |
| 2   | default-warmup-clip        | (in progress)      | (pending)| running  |
| 3   | default-fourier16          | (in progress)      | (pending)| running  |
| 4   | baseline-default           | (in progress)      | (pending)| running  |

## GPU usage

- 8 single-GPU jobs in parallel, each pinned to one GPU via
  `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES`.
- One wave was killed by OOM (h192/l8/mlp4 batch_size=4 fp32 didn't fit in 96 GB
  with full meshes); the wave 1b restart with `batch_size=2` worked. Wave 2's
  subsampling unblocks 8x bigger model dimensions in 23 GB instead of 47 GB peak.

## Next experiments (wave 4 backlog)

- Multi-seed (s=1, s=2) confirmation of the leading config.
- Lion optimizer at the AB-UPT recipe (lr=5e-5, wd=0.05, grad_clip=0.25, 100 ep).
- 200-epoch run on the leading config to see if the cosine tail keeps gaining.
- Smallest meaningful regularizer test: dropout=0.1 to see if there's any
  generalization gap on the unseen-camber tracks.
- Bigger model (n_hidden=256, n_layers=8, mlp_ratio=4) with the warmup+clip
  recipe and subsampling, longer than 100 epochs.
- Final candidate: best of wave 3 retrained from scratch for the test eval.

## Final recommendation (placeholder, finalized at end)

TBD.
"}

function.arguments:
{"path": "/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/scripts/wave3_b.sh", "content": "#!/bin/bash
# Wave 3 second batch — launches when wave 1 GPUs (0, 3, 4, 6, 7) free up.
# Run this after wave 1 finishes; it will overwrite GPU assignments.
set -e
cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."
mkdir -p session_logs

GROUP="mlintern-pai2-24h-v2-r1"
AGENT="ml-intern-r1"
WC="--warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0"
LR="--lr 1e-3"

start_run() {
  local gpu="$1"; shift
  local name="$1"; shift
  local logfile="session_logs/wave3-gpu${gpu}-${name}.log"
  echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] Launching GPU $gpu: $name → $logfile"
  CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="$gpu" SENPAI_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=720 \
    nohup python train.py \
      --agent "$AGENT" \
      --wandb_group "$GROUP" \
      --wandb_name "${GROUP}/${name}" \
      "$@" > "$logfile" 2>&1 &
  echo $! > "session_logs/wave3-gpu${gpu}-${name}.pid"
}

# GPU 0: best small + 100 epochs (no extras, single seed = control).
start_run 0 wc-lr1e3-100ep-seed0 \
  --epochs 100 $WC $LR --seed 0

# GPU 3: best small + Fourier + surf_weight=50 (combined extras).
start_run 3 wc-lr1e3-sw50-fourier-100ep-seed0 \
  --epochs 100 $WC $LR --seed 0 \
  --fourier_freq 16 --fourier_scale 5.0 \
  --surf_weight 50

# GPU 4: bigger model + sub32k + warmup-clip recipe (no Fourier).
start_run 4 wc-lr1e3-sub32k-h256-l8-mlp4-100ep \
  --epochs 100 $WC $LR \
  --subsample 32000 --surf_oversample 0.3 \
  --n_hidden 256 --n_layers 8 --n_head 8 --mlp_ratio 4

# GPU 6: bigger model + sub32k + Fourier + surf_weight=50 (full combo).
start_run 6 wc-lr1e3-sub32k-h256-l8-mlp4-fourier-sw50-100ep \
  --epochs 100 $WC $LR \
  --subsample 32000 --surf_oversample 0.3 \
  --n_hidden 256 --n_layers 8 --n_head 8 --mlp_ratio 4 \
  --fourier_freq 16 --fourier_scale 5.0 \
  --surf_weight 50

# GPU 7: best small + multi-seed (variance check).
start_run 7 wc-lr1e3-100ep-seed1 \
  --epochs 100 $WC $LR --seed 1

echo "Wave 3 second batch launched."
"}

{"command": "sleep 90 && cd /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/session_logs && for i in 0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7; do
  log=$(ls -t wave*-gpu${i}-*.log 2>/dev/null | head -1)

  curr=$(grep -E "^Epoch" "$log" | tail -1 | head -c 200)
  echo "GPU $i ($name): $curr"
done && date", "description": "Check progress"}

messages.content: There's a known bug in the test eval — `inf * 0 = nan` propagation from a single bad cruise test sample. Let me fix it in train.py without touching scoring.py:

function.arguments:
{"path": "/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/train.py", "old_str": "def evaluate_split(model, loader, stats, surf_weight, device, amp_dtype=None) -> dict[str, float]:
    """Run inference over a split and return metrics matching the organizer scorer."""
    vol_loss_sum = surf_loss_sum = 0.0
    mae_surf = torch.zeros(3, dtype=torch.float64, device=device)
    mae_vol = torch.zeros(3, dtype=torch.float64, device=device)
    n_surf = n_vol = n_batches = 0

    with torch.no_grad():
        for x, y, is_surface, mask in loader:
            x = x.to(device, non_blocking=True)
            y = y.to(device, non_blocking=True)
            is_surface = is_surface.to(device, non_blocking=True)
            mask = mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)

            x_norm = (x - stats["x_mean"]) / stats["x_std"]
            y_norm = (y - stats["y_mean"]) / stats["y_std"]
            if amp_dtype is not None:
                with torch.autocast(device_type="cuda", dtype=amp_dtype):
                    pred = model({"x": x_norm}, mask=mask)["preds"]
                pred = pred.float()
            else:
                pred = model({"x": x_norm}, mask=mask)["preds"]

            sq_err = (pred - y_norm) ** 2
            vol_mask = mask & ~is_surface
            surf_mask = mask & is_surface
            vol_loss_sum += (
                (sq_err * vol_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum()
                / vol_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
            ).item()
            surf_loss_sum += (
                (sq_err * surf_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum()
                / surf_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
            ).item()
            n_batches += 1

            pred_orig = pred * stats["y_std"] + stats["y_mean"]
            ds, dv = accumulate_batch(pred_orig, y, is_surface, mask, mae_surf, mae_vol)
            n_surf += ds
            n_vol += dv", "new_str": "def evaluate_split(model, loader, stats, surf_weight, device, amp_dtype=None) -> dict[str, float]:
    """Run inference over a split and return metrics matching the organizer scorer.

    Workaround for a known scoring.py edge case: at least one cruise test sample
    contains inf in its ground-truth y (test_geom_camber_cruise/000020.pt has
    761 inf values). ``accumulate_batch`` already tries to skip samples with
    non-finite y, but ``err = (pred - y).abs()`` is computed *before* the skip
    mask is applied, and ``inf * 0`` evaluates to ``nan`` in IEEE float — which
    then poisons the entire MAE sum and produces ``test_avg/mae_surf_p = nan``.
    The fix here is to filter bad samples out of the batch *before* calling
    ``accumulate_batch`` so the inf never enters the err computation. The
    semantics match what ``scoring.py`` claims to do (per-sample skip on
    non-finite y).
    """
    vol_loss_sum = surf_loss_sum = 0.0
    mae_surf = torch.zeros(3, dtype=torch.float64, device=device)
    mae_vol = torch.zeros(3, dtype=torch.float64, device=device)
    n_surf = n_vol = n_batches = 0

    with torch.no_grad():
        for x, y, is_surface, mask in loader:
            x = x.to(device, non_blocking=True)
            y = y.to(device, non_blocking=True)
            is_surface = is_surface.to(device, non_blocking=True)
            mask = mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)

            x_norm = (x - stats["x_mean"]) / stats["x_std"]
            y_norm = (y - stats["y_mean"]) / stats["y_std"]
            if amp_dtype is not None:
                with torch.autocast(device_type="cuda", dtype=amp_dtype):
                    pred = model({"x": x_norm}, mask=mask)["preds"]
                pred = pred.float()
            else:
                pred = model({"x": x_norm}, mask=mask)["preds"]

            # ---- normalized-space loss accounting (volume + surface) ----
            sq_err = (pred - y_norm) ** 2
            # NaN/Inf-safe: only count valid (mask=True) and finite y nodes.
            y_finite_node = torch.isfinite(y).all(dim=-1)  # [B, N]
            valid = mask & y_finite_node
            vol_mask = valid & ~is_surface
            surf_mask = valid & is_surface
            # Replace any non-finite predictions with 0 in the masked-out positions
            # so they cannot poison the sum. Since vol_mask/surf_mask are False
            # there, this multiplication doesn't affect the metric — but it
            # avoids inf*0 = nan.
            sq_err_safe = torch.where(valid.unsqueeze(-1), sq_err, torch.zeros_like(sq_err))
            vol_loss_sum += (
                (sq_err_safe * vol_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum()
                / vol_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
            ).item()
            surf_loss_sum += (
                (sq_err_safe * surf_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum()
                / surf_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
            ).item()
            n_batches += 1

            # ---- denormalized-space MAE accumulation (organizer-shared) ----
            pred_orig = pred * stats["y_std"] + stats["y_mean"]

            # Filter samples with any non-finite y BEFORE handing to accumulate_batch.
            # This preserves the per-sample skip semantics of scoring.py while
            # preventing inf from polluting the err computation inside.
            B = y.shape[0]
            y_finite_sample = torch.isfinite(y.reshape(B, -1)).all(dim=-1)  # [B]
            if y_finite_sample.any():
                good = y_finite_sample.nonzero(as_tuple=False).flatten()
                pred_g = pred_orig[good]
                y_g = y[good]
                is_g = is_surface[good]
                mk_g = mask[good]
                ds, dv = accumulate_batch(pred_g, y_g, is_g, mk_g, mae_surf, mae_vol)
                n_surf += ds
                n_vol += dv"}

function.arguments:
{"path": "/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/scripts/eval_test.py", "content": """"Load a saved checkpoint and re-evaluate val + test with the fixed eval code.

Usage:
  python scripts/eval_test.py --run_id <wandb-run-id> [--config config.yaml] [--gpu 0]

This is needed because runs that finished before the inf-handling fix in
train.py produced ``test_avg/mae_surf_p = nan`` whenever the cruise test split
contained sample 000020.pt (which has 761 inf values in y). The fix is in
``evaluate_split`` in train.py; this script re-runs that evaluation against
the saved best checkpoint so we get a real number out.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import os
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import torch
import yaml
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]))

from data import (
    TEST_SPLIT_NAMES,
    VAL_SPLIT_NAMES,
    X_DIM,
    aggregate_splits,
    load_data,
    load_test_data,
    pad_collate,
)
from train import Transolver, evaluate_split


def main():
    p = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    p.add_argument("--run_id", required=True, help="wandb run id (model-{run_id} dir name)")
    p.add_argument("--gpu", type=int, default=0)
    p.add_argument("--splits_dir", default="/mnt/new-pvc/datasets/tandemfoil/splits_v2")
    p.add_argument("--batch_size", type=int, default=4)
    p.add_argument("--surf_weight", type=float, default=10.0)
    p.add_argument("--out", default=None, help="optional JSON output path")
    args = p.parse_args()

    os.environ.setdefault("CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES", str(args.gpu))
    device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")

    model_dir = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] / f"models/model-{args.run_id}"
    cfg_path = model_dir / "config.yaml"
    ckpt_path = model_dir / "checkpoint.pt"

    if not cfg_path.exists() or not ckpt_path.exists():
        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Missing model dir: {model_dir}")

    with cfg_path.open() as f:
        model_cfg = yaml.safe_load(f)
    print(f"Model config: {model_cfg}")

    train_ds, val_splits, stats, _ = load_data(args.splits_dir, debug=False)
    stats = {k: v.to(device) for k, v in stats.items()}

    loader_kwargs = dict(collate_fn=pad_collate, num_workers=4, pin_memory=True,
                         persistent_workers=True, prefetch_factor=2)
    val_loaders = {name: DataLoader(ds, batch_size=args.batch_size, shuffle=False, **loader_kwargs)
                   for name, ds in val_splits.items()}

    model = Transolver(**model_cfg).to(device)
    state = torch.load(ckpt_path, map_location=device, weights_only=True)
    model.load_state_dict(state)
    model.eval()
    n_params = sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters())
    print(f"Loaded checkpoint with {n_params/1e6:.2f}M params")

    # Val
    val_metrics = {name: evaluate_split(model, l, stats, args.surf_weight, device)
                   for name, l in val_loaders.items()}
    val_avg = aggregate_splits(val_metrics)
    print(f"VAL avg/mae_surf_p = {val_avg['avg/mae_surf_p']:.4f}")
    for n, m in val_metrics.items():
        print(f"  {n:<26s} surf_p={m['mae_surf_p']:.4f} surf_Ux={m['mae_surf_Ux']:.4f} surf_Uy={m['mae_surf_Uy']:.4f}")

    # Test
    print()
    test_datasets = load_test_data(args.splits_dir, debug=False)
    test_loaders = {name: DataLoader(ds, batch_size=args.batch_size, shuffle=False, **loader_kwargs)
                    for name, ds in test_datasets.items()}
    test_metrics = {name: evaluate_split(model, l, stats, args.surf_weight, device)
                    for name, l in test_loaders.items()}
    test_avg = aggregate_splits(test_metrics)
    print(f"TEST avg/mae_surf_p = {test_avg['avg/mae_surf_p']:.4f}")
    for n, m in test_metrics.items():
        print(f"  {n:<26s} surf_p={m['mae_surf_p']:.4f} surf_Ux={m['mae_surf_Ux']:.4f} surf_Uy={m['mae_surf_Uy']:.4f}")

    if args.out:
        out = {
            "run_id": args.run_id,
            "model_config": model_cfg,
            "val_avg": val_avg,
            "test_avg": test_avg,
            "val_per_split": val_metrics,
            "test_per_split": test_metrics,
        }
        Path(args.out).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        Path(args.out).write_text(json.dumps(out, default=str, indent=2))
        print(f"Wrote {args.out}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
"}

{"command": "# Launch wave 3 second batch on GPUs 0, 3, 4
cd /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target

GROUP="mlintern-pai2-24h-v2-r1"
AGENT="ml-intern-r1"
WC="--warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0"
LR="--lr 1e-3"

# GPU 0: best small + 100 epochs (extended, control).
CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 SENPAI_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=720 \
  nohup python train.py \
    --epochs 100 $WC $LR --seed 0 \
    --agent "$AGENT" \
    --wandb_group "$GROUP" \
    --wandb_name "${GROUP}/wc-lr1e3-100ep-seed0" \
    > session_logs/wave3-gpu0-wc-lr1e3-100ep-seed0.log 2>&1 & echo $! > /tmp/w3g0.pid

# GPU 3: best small + Fourier + surf_weight=50.
CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=3 SENPAI_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=720 \
  nohup python train.py \
    --epochs 100 $WC $LR --seed 0 \
    --fourier_freq 16 --fourier_scale 5.0 \
    --surf_weight 50 \
    --agent "$AGENT" \
    --wandb_group "$GROUP" \
    --wandb_name "${GROUP}/wc-lr1e3-sw50-fourier-100ep-seed0" \
    > session_logs/wave3-gpu3-wc-lr1e3-sw50-fourier-100ep-seed0.log 2>&1 & echo $! > /tmp/w3g3.pid

# GPU 4: bigger model + sub32k + warmup-clip recipe (no Fourier).
CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=4 SENPAI_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=720 \
  nohup python train.py \
    --epochs 100 $WC $LR \
    --subsample 32000 --surf_oversample 0.3 \
    --n_hidden 256 --n_layers 8 --n_head 8 --mlp_ratio 4 \
    --agent "$AGENT" \
    --wandb_group "$GROUP" \
    --wandb_name "${GROUP}/wc-lr1e3-sub32k-h256-l8-mlp4-100ep" \
    > session_logs/wave3-gpu4-wc-lr1e3-sub32k-h256-l8-mlp4-100ep.log 2>&1 & echo $! > /tmp/w3g4.pid

sleep 15
echo "=== GPU 0 ==="
tail -3 session_logs/wave3-gpu0-*.log 2>/dev/null
echo "=== GPU 3 ==="
tail -3 session_logs/wave3-gpu3-*.log 2>/dev/null
echo "=== GPU 4 ==="
tail -3 session_logs/wave3-gpu4-*.log 2>/dev/null
nvidia-smi --query-gpu=index,memory.used --format=csv 2>&1 | head -10", "description": "Launch wave 3 second batch"}

messages.content: GPU 6, 7 finished. Launch last 2 wave 3 jobs and re-eval the finished checkpoints with the test-fix:

function.arguments:
{"command": "# Launch last 2 wave 3 jobs on GPU 6, 7
cd /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target

GROUP="mlintern-pai2-24h-v2-r1"
AGENT="ml-intern-r1"
WC="--warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0"
LR="--lr 1e-3"

# GPU 6: bigger model + sub32k + Fourier + surf_weight=50 (full combo).
CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=6 SENPAI_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=720 \
  nohup python train.py \
    --epochs 100 $WC $LR \
    --subsample 32000 --surf_oversample 0.3 \
    --n_hidden 256 --n_layers 8 --n_head 8 --mlp_ratio 4 \
    --fourier_freq 16 --fourier_scale 5.0 \
    --surf_weight 50 \
    --agent "$AGENT" \
    --wandb_group "$GROUP" \
    --wandb_name "${GROUP}/wc-lr1e3-sub32k-h256-l8-mlp4-fourier-sw50-100ep" \
    > session_logs/wave3-gpu6-wc-lr1e3-sub32k-h256-l8-mlp4-fourier-sw50-100ep.log 2>&1 & echo $! > /tmp/w3g6.pid

# GPU 7: best small + multi-seed s=1.
CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=7 SENPAI_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=720 \
  nohup python train.py \
    --epochs 100 $WC $LR --seed 1 \
    --agent "$AGENT" \
    --wandb_group "$GROUP" \
    --wandb_name "${GROUP}/wc-lr1e3-100ep-seed1" \
    > session_logs/wave3-gpu7-wc-lr1e3-100ep-seed1.log 2>&1 & echo $! > /tmp/w3g7.pid

sleep 15
echo "=== GPU 6 ==="
tail -3 session_logs/wave3-gpu6-wc-lr1e3-sub32k-h256-l8-mlp4-fourier-sw50-100ep.log
echo "=== GPU 7 ==="
tail -3 session_logs/wave3-gpu7-wc-lr1e3-100ep-seed1.log
nvidia-smi --query-gpu=index,memory.used --format=csv 2>&1 | head -10", "description": "Launch last wave 3 jobs"}
function.name: bash

function.arguments:
{"command": "cd /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target && git config user.email "ml-intern@anthropic.com" 2>/dev/null; git config user.name "ML Intern" 2>/dev/null; git commit -m "ml-intern: framework + waves 1-3 of TandemFoilSet experiments

Make train.py configurable from the CLI (model size, optimizer, schedule,
warmup, grad-clip, AMP, surface weights, Fourier features, Transolver++
ablations, mesh subsampling, optional Lion). Add a NaN/Inf-safe path in
evaluate_split that filters samples with non-finite y before calling the
shared scoring.accumulate_batch; a single cruise test sample with 761 inf
values was producing test_avg/mae_surf_p = nan via inf*0 = nan.

Adds:
  scripts/{wave1,wave1b,wave1c,wave2,wave3,wave3_b}.sh — experiment launchers
  scripts/collect_results.py — log → JSONL summary collector
  scripts/eval_test.py — re-evaluate any saved checkpoint with the fix
  research/MLINTERN_SUMMARY.md, MLINTERN_RESULTS.jsonl — running summary

Best so far on this branch: \`default-lr1e3-warmup-clip\` (50 epochs)
val_avg/mae_surf_p ~51 vs vanilla baseline ~80." 2>&1 | tail -10", "description": "Commit progress"}

    print("\nRun summary:")

    for r in rows:

        ep = best.get("epoch") or 0

function.arguments:
{"path": "/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/research/MLINTERN_SUMMARY.md", "old_str": "## TL;DR

The leading recipe is the baseline Transolver (n_hidden=128, n_layers=5, n_head=4, slice_num=64,
mlp_ratio=2) trained with **5 % linear warmup + cosine decay + grad-clip 1.0 at lr=1e-3**.
That single 50-epoch run reached `val_avg/mae_surf_p ≈ 73` mid-run vs ~140 for the
unmodified baseline at the same epoch. Final test metric is filled in below once the run
completes its end-of-run test pass.", "new_str": "## TL;DR

The leading recipe is the **baseline Transolver** (n_hidden=128, n_layers=5,
n_head=4, slice_num=64, mlp_ratio=2) trained with **5 % linear warmup + cosine
decay + grad-clip 1.0 at lr=1e-3** for 50 epochs.

Best 50-epoch run so far:

| Run | val_avg/mae_surf_p | test_avg/mae_surf_p |
|-----|-------------------:|--------------------:|
| `default-lr1e3-warmup-clip` (best) | **51.05** | **44.19** |
| `default-warmup-clip` (lr=5e-4)    | 53.73 | 46.59 |
| `baseline-default` (control)       | 80.68 | 72.93 |
| `default-fourier16`                | 85.52 | 75.11 |

Current 100-epoch wave 3 runs in progress will likely push lower; the big-model
variant (`wc-lr1e3-sub32k-h256-l8-mlp4-fourier-100ep`) is already at val~69 at
epoch 38 of 100.

**`scoring.py` inf bug**: `data/scoring.py` is read-only per the contract. I
hit a corner-case: `test_geom_camber_cruise/000020.pt` has 761 inf values in
its ground-truth y, and the `inf*0 = nan` propagation poisons the test sum so
the in-training test eval prints `test_avg=nan`. Worked around by filtering
non-finite samples out of the batch *before* calling `accumulate_batch`
inside `evaluate_split` in `train.py` — semantics unchanged (same per-sample
skip rule that `scoring.py` already documents). All test numbers in this
summary come from this fix; `scripts/eval_test.py` re-runs evaluation from
saved checkpoints with the fix applied."}

{"path": "/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/scripts/wave4.sh", "content": "#!/bin/bash
# Wave 4: launched when wave 3 finishes (~01:00 UTC).
#
# What we know from waves 1-3:
#   - default model + warmup_frac=0.05 + grad_clip=1.0 + lr=1e-3 → val 51 / test 44
#   - The recipe is robust; warmup-clip without lr=1e-3 (default 5e-4) → val 54 / test 47
#   - Bigger model (sub32k+h256+l8+mlp4) on 100 epochs converges slower; ep46≈64
#   - 100-epoch schedules of small model are still in early phase at ep10-13
#
# Wave 4 priorities:
#   1. Multi-seed of the proven 50-epoch best (seeds 2, 3, 4) — variance + ensemble.
#   2. Best 50ep config + Fourier (with the same warmup-clip recipe).
#   3. 200-epoch run of best small config (seed 0) on one GPU.
#   4. Lion optimizer exploration on best small recipe (lr=5e-5).
#   5. Mid-size model (h160, l6, mlp3) without subsampling — capacity bump.
#   6. Reserve 1 GPU for a final ensemble re-evaluation script.
set -e
cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."
mkdir -p session_logs

GROUP="mlintern-pai2-24h-v2-r1"
AGENT="ml-intern-r1"
WC="--warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0"
LR1E3="--lr 1e-3"

start_run() {
  local gpu="$1"; shift
  local name="$1"; shift
  local logfile="session_logs/wave4-gpu${gpu}-${name}.log"
  echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] Launching GPU $gpu: $name → $logfile"
  CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="$gpu" SENPAI_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=720 \
    nohup python train.py \
      --agent "$AGENT" \
      --wandb_group "$GROUP" \
      --wandb_name "${GROUP}/${name}" \
      "$@" > "$logfile" 2>&1 &
  echo $! > "session_logs/wave4-gpu${gpu}-${name}.pid"
}

# ---------- Multi-seed of proven 50-ep best (variance + ensemble) ----------
start_run 0 wc-lr1e3-50ep-seed2 \
  --epochs 50 $WC $LR1E3 --seed 2

start_run 1 wc-lr1e3-50ep-seed3 \
  --epochs 50 $WC $LR1E3 --seed 3

start_run 2 wc-lr1e3-50ep-seed4 \
  --epochs 50 $WC $LR1E3 --seed 4

# ---------- 50-ep best + Fourier features ----------
start_run 3 wc-lr1e3-50ep-fourier-seed0 \
  --epochs 50 $WC $LR1E3 --seed 0 \
  --fourier_freq 16 --fourier_scale 5.0

# ---------- 50-ep best + a moderate surf_weight bump (sw=20) ----------
start_run 4 wc-lr1e3-50ep-sw20-seed0 \
  --epochs 50 $WC $LR1E3 --seed 0 \
  --surf_weight 20

# ---------- Lion optimizer experiment ----------
# AB-UPT recipe: lr=5e-5, weight_decay=0.05, grad_clip=0.25.
start_run 5 wc-lion-lr5e5-50ep \
  --epochs 50 \
  --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 0.25 \
  --optimizer lion --lr 5e-5 --weight_decay 0.05 \
  --seed 0

# ---------- 200-epoch run of best small config (seed 0) ----------
start_run 6 wc-lr1e3-200ep-seed0 \
  --epochs 200 $WC $LR1E3 --seed 0

# ---------- best recipe + h128 l8 mlp2 (slight capacity bump, no subsampling, batch=2) ----------
start_run 7 wc-lr1e3-h128-l8-mlp2-bs2-50ep \
  --epochs 50 $WC $LR1E3 --seed 0 \
  --n_hidden 128 --n_layers 8 --n_head 4 --mlp_ratio 2 \
  --batch_size 2

echo "Wave 4 launched."
"}

function.arguments:
{"path": "/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/scripts/eval_ensemble.py", "content": """"Ensemble evaluation: average predictions from multiple checkpoints.

For each batch, run the forward pass for every model and average the
*denormalized* predictions before computing MAE. This is the standard
test-time ensemble for regression models.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import os
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import torch
import yaml
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]))

from data import (
    TEST_SPLIT_NAMES,
    VAL_SPLIT_NAMES,
    accumulate_batch,
    aggregate_splits,
    finalize_split,
    load_data,
    load_test_data,
    pad_collate,
)
from train import Transolver


def load_model(model_dir: Path, device: torch.device) -> Transolver:
    cfg = yaml.safe_load((model_dir / "config.yaml").open())
    model = Transolver(**cfg).to(device)
    state = torch.load(model_dir / "checkpoint.pt", map_location=device, weights_only=True)
    model.load_state_dict(state)
    model.eval()
    return model


@torch.no_grad()
def evaluate_ensemble(models, loader, stats, device):
    mae_surf = torch.zeros(3, dtype=torch.float64, device=device)
    mae_vol = torch.zeros(3, dtype=torch.float64, device=device)
    n_surf = n_vol = 0

    for x, y, is_surface, mask in loader:
        x = x.to(device, non_blocking=True)
        y = y.to(device, non_blocking=True)
        is_surface = is_surface.to(device, non_blocking=True)
        mask = mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)

        x_norm = (x - stats["x_mean"]) / stats["x_std"]
        # Average the denormalized predictions across models.
        pred_orig_avg = None
        for m in models:
            pred = m({"x": x_norm}, mask=mask)["preds"]
            pred_orig = pred * stats["y_std"] + stats["y_mean"]
            if pred_orig_avg is None:
                pred_orig_avg = pred_orig.clone()
            else:
                pred_orig_avg += pred_orig
        pred_orig_avg /= len(models)

        # Filter samples with non-finite y (avoids inf*0 = nan)
        B = y.shape[0]
        y_finite = torch.isfinite(y.reshape(B, -1)).all(dim=-1)
        if y_finite.any():
            good = y_finite.nonzero(as_tuple=False).flatten()
            ds, dv = accumulate_batch(
                pred_orig_avg[good], y[good], is_surface[good], mask[good], mae_surf, mae_vol
            )
            n_surf += ds
            n_vol += dv

    return finalize_split(mae_surf, mae_vol, n_surf, n_vol)


def main():
    p = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    p.add_argument("--run_ids", nargs="+", required=True, help="wandb run ids of checkpoints to ensemble")
    p.add_argument("--gpu", type=int, default=0)
    p.add_argument("--splits_dir", default="/mnt/new-pvc/datasets/tandemfoil/splits_v2")
    p.add_argument("--batch_size", type=int, default=2)
    p.add_argument("--out", default=None)
    args = p.parse_args()

    os.environ.setdefault("CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES", str(args.gpu))
    device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")

    base = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] / "models"
    models = []
    for rid in args.run_ids:
        model_dir = base / f"model-{rid}"
        if not model_dir.exists():
            raise FileNotFoundError(model_dir)
        models.append(load_model(model_dir, device))
        print(f"Loaded {rid}")
    print(f"Ensemble of {len(models)} models")

    train_ds, val_splits, stats, _ = load_data(args.splits_dir, debug=False)
    stats = {k: v.to(device) for k, v in stats.items()}

    loader_kwargs = dict(collate_fn=pad_collate, num_workers=4, pin_memory=True,
                         persistent_workers=True, prefetch_factor=2)

    val_loaders = {n: DataLoader(d, batch_size=args.batch_size, shuffle=False, **loader_kwargs)
                   for n, d in val_splits.items()}
    val_metrics = {n: evaluate_ensemble(models, l, stats, device) for n, l in val_loaders.items()}
    val_avg = aggregate_splits(val_metrics)
    print(f"VAL ensemble avg/mae_surf_p = {val_avg['avg/mae_surf_p']:.4f}")
    for n, m in val_metrics.items():
        print(f"  {n:<26s} surf_p={m['mae_surf_p']:.4f}")

    print()
    test_datasets = load_test_data(args.splits_dir, debug=False)
    test_loaders = {n: DataLoader(d, batch_size=args.batch_size, shuffle=False, **loader_kwargs)
                    for n, d in test_datasets.items()}
    test_metrics = {n: evaluate_ensemble(models, l, stats, device) for n, l in test_loaders.items()}
    test_avg = aggregate_splits(test_metrics)
    print(f"TEST ensemble avg/mae_surf_p = {test_avg['avg/mae_surf_p']:.4f}")
    for n, m in test_metrics.items():
        print(f"  {n:<26s} surf_p={m['mae_surf_p']:.4f}")

    if args.out:
        Path(args.out).write_text(json.dumps({
            "run_ids": args.run_ids,
            "val_avg": val_avg,
            "test_avg": test_avg,
            "val_per_split": val_metrics,
            "test_per_split": test_metrics,
        }, default=str, indent=2))
        print(f"Wrote {args.out}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
"}

{"command": "sleep 240 && cd /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/session_logs && for i in 0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7; do
  log=$(ls -t wave*-gpu${i}-*.log 2>/dev/null | head -1)
  [ -z "$log" ] && continue
  name=$(echo "$log" | sed -E 's/^wave[a-z0-9]+-gpu[0-9]-//; s/\.log$//')
  curr=$(grep -E "^Epoch" "$log" | tail -1 | head -c 200)
  echo "GPU $i ($name): $curr"
done && date", "description": "Wait 4 min"}

  echo "GPU $i ($name): $best"
done && date", "description": "Status"}

{"command": "sleep 110 && cd /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/session_logs && for i in 0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7; do
  log=$(ls -t wave*-gpu${i}-*.log 2>/dev/null | head -1)
  [ -z "$log" ] && continue
  name=$(echo "$log" | sed -E 's/^wave[a-z0-9]+-gpu[0-9]-//; s/\.log$//')
  best=$(grep -E "^Epoch.*\*|TEST|Logged|Training done" "$log" | tail -1 | head -c 200)
  echo "GPU $i ($name): $best"
done && date", "description": "Status"}

# GPU 2: sw50+wc+lr1e3 100ep seed=1 (multi-seed of new champion)

    --epochs 100 \

function.arguments:
{"command": "cd /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target

GROUP="mlintern-pai2-24h-v2-r1"
AGENT="ml-intern-r1"

# GPU 1: another seed of new champion (sw50+wc+lr1e3 100ep small) seed=2 (variance check)
CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 SENPAI_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=720 \
  nohup python train.py \
    --epochs 100 \
    --warmup_frac 0.05 --grad_clip 1.0 --lr 1e-3 \
    --surf_weight 50 \
    --seed 2 \
    --agent "$AGENT" \
    --wandb_group "$GROUP" \
    --wandb_name "${GROUP}/wc-lr1e3-sw50-100ep-seed2" \
    > session_logs/wave4-gpu1-wc-lr1e3-sw50-100ep-seed2.log 2>&1 & echo $! > /tmp/w4g1.pid

sleep 10
tail -3 session_logs/wave4-gpu1-wc-lr1e3-sw50-100ep-seed2.log
echo "=== Status ==="
nvidia-smi --query-gpu=index,memory.used --format=csv 2>&1 | head -10", "description": "Launch wave 4 sw50 seed=2"}

function.arguments:
{"command": "sleep 110 && cd /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/session_logs && for i in 0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7; do
  log=$(ls -t wave*-gpu${i}-*.log 2>/dev/null | head -1)
  [ -z "$log" ] && continue
  name=$(echo "$log" | sed -E 's/^wave[a-z0-9]+-gpu[0-9]-//; s/\.log$//')
  best=$(grep -E "^Epoch.*\*|TEST|Logged|Training done" "$log" | tail -1 | head -c 200)
  echo "GPU $i ($name): $best"
done && date", "description": "Status"}

function.arguments:
{"path": "/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/research/MLINTERN_SUMMARY.md", "old_str": "## TL;DR

The leading recipe is the **baseline Transolver** (n_hidden=128, n_layers=5,
n_head=4, slice_num=64, mlp_ratio=2) trained with **5 % linear warmup + cosine
decay + grad-clip 1.0 at lr=1e-3** for 50 epochs.

Best 50-epoch run so far:

| Run | val_avg/mae_surf_p | test_avg/mae_surf_p |
|-----|-------------------:|--------------------:|
| `default-lr1e3-warmup-clip` (best) | **51.05** | **44.19** |
| `default-warmup-clip` (lr=5e-4)    | 53.73 | 46.59 |
| `baseline-default` (control)       | 80.68 | 72.93 |
| `default-fourier16`                | 85.52 | 75.11 |

Current 100-epoch wave 3 runs in progress will likely push lower; the big-model
variant (`wc-lr1e3-sub32k-h256-l8-mlp4-fourier-100ep`) is already at val~69 at
epoch 38 of 100.", "new_str": "## TL;DR

The leading recipe is the **baseline Transolver** (n_hidden=128, n_layers=5,
n_head=4, slice_num=64, mlp_ratio=2) trained with **5 % linear warmup + cosine
decay + grad-clip 1.0 at lr=1e-3** for **100 epochs**.

The single best run is `wc-lr1e3-100ep-seed0` (run id `necjs23h`):

| Run | val_avg/mae_surf_p | test_avg/mae_surf_p |
|-----|-------------------:|--------------------:|
| **5-model ensemble** (best 5 wave 3 checkpoints, see below) | **34.33** | **29.33** |
| 2-seed ensemble (`necjs23h` + `pcatusyd`)                   | 36.75 | 31.74 |
| `wc-lr1e3-100ep-seed0` (single best)                        | 39.61 | 34.21 |
| `wc-lr1e3-100ep-seed1`                                      | 39.94 | 34.98 |
| `wc-lr1e3-sw50-100ep-seed0`                                 | 40.50 | 35.34 |
| `wc-lr1e3-sub32k-h256-l8-mlp4-100ep` (big-model variant)    | 40.59 | 35.64 |
| `wc-lr1e3-sub32k-h256-l8-mlp4-fourier-100ep`                | 42.79 | 36.37 |
| `default-lr1e3-warmup-clip` (50 ep, wave 1 baseline)        | 51.05 | 44.19 |
| `baseline-default` (50 ep, vanilla control)                 | 80.68 | 72.93 |

Bottom line: at the same data, same architecture, same optimizer, the difference
between the unmodified baseline and our best **single** run is `72.93 → 34.21`
(53 % reduction in surface-pressure MAE) just from `lr=1e-3 + 5 % warmup +
grad-clip 1.0 + 100 epochs`. The 5-model ensemble pushes this to `29.33`
(60 % reduction).

The ensemble averages predictions from 5 diverse 100-epoch checkpoints:
`necjs23h, pcatusyd, nhj06b8t, rnb24j1e, 8s5o27g0` (small seeds 0/1, small +
sw=50, big sub32k, big sub32k + fourier).

**Surprise findings**:
- Surface-loss-weighted (sw=50, AirfRANS-recommended) helped *less* than just
  doubling the schedule from 50 → 100 epochs.
- Bigger model with mesh subsampling matches but does not beat the small model
  trained for 100 epochs.
- Fourier features + sw=50 *together* hurt — the two regularizers compose
  poorly with this dataset's already-rich shape descriptors.
- Transolver++ (Ada-Temp + Rep-Slice) NaN'd unless temperature is clamped;
  even after the clamp, it did not improve over vanilla within 50 epochs."}

  curr_ep=$(grep -E "^Epoch " "$log" | tail -1 | awk '{print $2}')
  status=$(grep -E "Training done|TEST  avg|Logged" "$log" | tail -1 | head -c 60)
  echo "GPU $i ($name): ep=$curr_ep, best=$best, status=$status"
done && date", "description": "Wait"}
function.name: bash
